name: ADHD Spark
description: Your attention is a hummingbird with a deadline. It darts, fixates, abandons, and circles back — catching details everyone else walked past and losing the thread everyone else held. Improvisation is your native tongue.
flavorText: Six tabs open in your skull, three of them screaming, one of them brilliant. Good luck guessing which.
effect: The holder notices overlooked details and improvises wildly, but struggles to stay on task. Use it for sudden insight and lateral problem-solving; let focus scatter at dramatically inconvenient and convenient moments alike.
artPrompt: A head wreathed in a swirling constellation of darting light-trails branching in every direction at once, a few sparks flaring brilliant, others fizzling, pure kinetic scatter. No object. Bold electric multicolor against soft dark, crisp clean linework, restless brilliant energy, simple background.

name: Bad Luck Charm
description: Misfortune orbits you like a moon. Things break, plans curdle, ceilings leak — but here's the secret nobody expects: the bad luck splashes outward, soaking everyone in the room, often more than you.
flavorText: Disaster-prone, yes, but you've learned to stand next to the people you'd like disaster to find first.
effect: The holder attracts mishaps that frequently strike bystanders and enemies as much as themselves. Weaponize the spillover — position the holder so the chaos lands where it's useful. Comedy through collateral fortune.
artPrompt: A figure-shaped void at the center of a slow-motion explosion of small calamities — a falling flowerpot, a snapping rope, a startled cat — all radiating outward away from the center. No literal person. Muted tones with chaotic accent-colors, crisp linework, comic doom, simple background.

name: Death Wish
description: You take risks that make sensible people leave the room. Not because you want to die — because fear stopped getting your attention years ago. This makes you very hard to intimidate and very tiring to befriend.
flavorText: Threats bounce off you like rain off a statue. The statue is grinning. That's the part that worries people.
effect: The holder is nearly fearless and takes reckless risks, becoming extremely hard to intimidate or coerce. Use the audacity for bold plays; let the absence of self-preservation create as much trouble as triumph.
artPrompt: A silhouette leaning calmly off the edge of a great height, arms loose, posture relaxed into the danger, wind tearing past, utterly unbothered. No object. High-contrast color with a vertiginous drop, crisp bold linework, serene recklessness, simple background.

name: Heavy Metal Musician
description: You can shred, scream, and bend a crowd's mood like hot iron. A tavern becomes a riot or a ritual depending on the riff. The power is real; the volume is a feature, not an apology.
flavorText: Turned a funeral into a moshpit once. Mourners said it was what the deceased would have wanted. The deceased had said no such thing.
effect: The holder commands crowds and atmosphere through raw musical power — inciting frenzy, summoning catharsis, drowning out the unwanted. Use it to swing the energy of any gathering hard in either direction.
artPrompt: An explosion of sound made visible — jagged soundwave-lightning erupting outward in a storm of sparks and shattering air, raw kinetic noise with no instrument shown. Searing red-and-purple with white flares, crisp aggressive linework, deafening energy, simple background.

name: Lucky Penny
description: You're lucky in small, specific, plot-relevant ways. Not jackpots — the right coin in your pocket, the green light, the dropped clue that lands face-up. Minor fortune, exquisitely well-timed.
flavorText: Never wins the lottery. Always finds the parking spot. The universe deals in small mercies and you collect them all.
effect: The holder enjoys minor, well-timed strokes of luck — small useful coincidences exactly when the scene needs nudging. Keep it modest and frequent; it greases moments rather than rewriting them.
artPrompt: A single glowing coin spinning at the heart of a soft golden ripple of fortune, tiny sparkles cascading off it, warm and humble rather than grand. No figure. Warm gold-and-copper glow, crisp clean linework, quiet good fortune, simple background.

name: Lupus
description: A chronic condition you carry through every adventure — flares, fatigue, and the daily negotiation between what you want to do and what your body will permit. It brings vulnerability, hard-won resilience, and complications no quest accounted for.
flavorText: The dragon is a Tuesday problem. The real boss fight is a flare at the worst possible moment, and you've beaten it before.
effect: The holder manages a real chronic illness — unpredictable flares and fatigue that complicate plans, balanced by deep resilience and adaptive strength. Treat it with dignity; let it shape pacing and grit, never as a punchline.
artPrompt: A delicate butterfly (the lupus symbol) rendered in warm light across an abstract figure-shape, one wing bright and strong, one wing faintly storm-touched, dignified and luminous. No literal person. Warm rose-gold with soft storm-grey, crisp gentle linework, resilient and tender, simple background.

name: Minor Stage Magic
description: Sleight of hand, misdirection, theatrical flourish. No real magic — just decades of practice making the impossible look casual. People watch your left hand. The interesting work happens in your right.
flavorText: The trick is not the trick. The trick is that you've already done the trick while they were admiring the cape.
effect: The holder excels at sleight of hand, misdirection, and showmanship — palming objects, redirecting attention, faking the supernatural. Use it for cons, escapes, and convincing people they saw something they didn't.
artPrompt: A pair of hands mid-flourish with a card or coin half-vanished between them, a fan of misdirecting sparkles drawing the eye one way while the real action slips the other. No face. Rich theatrical purple-and-gold, crisp elegant linework, showman's flair, simple background.

name: Quiet Courage
description: You are not fearless. You are afraid, often, deeply — and you keep going anyway. This is the rarer and sturdier kind of brave: the sort that shows up, shaking, and does the thing regardless.
flavorText: No war cry. No dramatic speech. Just one steady step forward when every cell is voting to run.
effect: The holder pushes through fear with unshowy persistence — steady under pressure, reliable when others freeze. Not flashy; let it shine in the moments that require enduring rather than charging.
artPrompt: A small, steady flame standing upright against a buffeting dark wind, bent but unextinguished, casting a brave little circle of warm light. No figure. Warm amber flame against cold dark blue, crisp clean linework, understated bravery, simple background.

name: Ritual Precision
description: You rely on strict habits, patterns, and checks — the same order, the same way, every time. To others it looks like quirk. To you it's an early-warning system: when the pattern breaks, you know, instantly, that something is wrong.
flavorText: Three taps, always. The day you only manage two, start running. The pattern knows before you do.
effect: The holder's rigid routines double as a tripwire for anomalies — they immediately sense when something in their environment is off. Use it to detect tampering, impostors, and wrongness others overlook.
artPrompt: A precise geometric mandala of repeating symbols and grid-lines, perfectly ordered except for one tiny broken element glowing red with wrongness. No figure. Cool ordered blues with one alarm-red break, crisp meticulous linework, tense precision, simple background.

name: Secretive Backstory
description: You have missing years, old enemies, and a list of questions you simply will not answer. The blanks are deliberate. The past is a locked room and you've swallowed the key and several follow-up keys.
flavorText: 'It's complicated' is the most you'll offer. It is, in fact, far more complicated than even that admits.
effect: The holder has a deliberately obscured past that surfaces at dramatic moments — unexpected skills, old foes, sudden recognition. Use the mystery as a renewable source of plot; the holder reveals on their own terms, or not.
artPrompt: A figure-shape rendered as a locked silhouette, most of it in deep shadow, a single old photograph or document torn and half-burned drifting at the edge, secrets implied. No clear person. Moody noir tones with one warm clue-glow, crisp clean linework, enigmatic, simple background.

name: Streetwise
description: You know who to ask, where to hide, and exactly when to run. Years on the pavement taught you to read a situation in seconds — the safe alley, the wrong crowd, the deal that's about to go bad.
flavorText: The city talks. You're one of the few people fluent in everything it isn't saying out loud.
effect: The holder reads urban situations expertly — finding safe routes, reliable contacts, and danger before it lands. Use it for navigation, information, and the instinct to leave a room one beat before it turns.
artPrompt: A maze of neon-lit alleyways from above, glowing paths threading through the dark showing the safe way out, hazards marked in red at the corners. No figure. Gritty neon color against urban shadow, crisp clean linework, savvy and alert, simple background.

name: Animal Magnetism
description: Beasts trust you before people do. Dogs cross streets to reach you, wary horses settle, wild things pause. There's no spell to it — animals simply read something in you that people are too clever to notice.
flavorText: Cats that hate everyone tolerate you. This is a more impressive credential than most résumés.
effect: The holder instinctively wins the trust of animals — calming, befriending, and recruiting creatures. Use it for mounts, allies, and the social advantage of being the one a suspicious beast chooses to like.
artPrompt: A warm radiant aura at the center, with varied animals — bird, fox, cat, large beast — leaning inward toward it from all sides, calm and drawn. No human figure. Warm gold glow with natural animal tones, crisp gentle linework, magnetic warmth, simple background.

name: Buddhist Peace
description: You stay calm where others shatter. Threats arrive and find no purchase; panic spreads through a room and stops at the edge of your stillness. That steadiness is contagious — it can talk a mob down from itself.
flavorText: The storm rages. You are the eye. The eye does not negotiate with the storm; it simply is where the storm is not.
effect: The holder remains serene under threat and radiates a calm that can soothe others and defuse panic. Use it to de-escalate, to think clearly amid chaos, and to anchor allies who are losing their nerve.
artPrompt: A single still pond reflecting a calm sky at the center of a raging surrounding storm, the water perfectly unrippled, radiating quiet outward. No figure. Serene blues and warm light against turbulent grey, crisp clean linework, profound stillness, simple background.

name: Burner Identity
description: You maintain a complete backup self — name, outfit, papers, reputation, a small history that holds up to questioning. When the current you becomes a liability, you simply step into the other you and walk away clean.
flavorText: Two people, one body, both behind on their library books. Only one of them is wanted by name.
effect: The holder can assume a fully-prepared alternate identity to evade pursuit or access restricted situations. Use it for clean escapes and infiltration; the burner self is convincing but finite — each use risks burning it.
artPrompt: Two overlapping ID-card silhouettes, one dissolving into smoke as the other sharpens into focus, a clean swap mid-transition. No real face. Cool greys with one warm emerging accent, crisp clean linework, slick and clandestine, simple background.

name: Con Artist's Smile
description: You can bluff your way through doors, debts, and suspicious guards on charm and confidence alone. The smile does most of the work. It's warm, it's certain, and it has never once been backed by the documents it implies exist.
flavorText: Sincerity is a skill, and yours is entirely fraudulent and entirely convincing. The two are not in tension.
effect: The holder talks past obstacles through sheer confident charm — bluffing officials, smoothing suspicion, selling the story. Use it for social infiltration and fast-talking; it works until someone actually checks.
artPrompt: A dazzling, disarming smile rendered as the focal point with charm-sparkles radiating from it, a faint shadow behind suggesting the bluff. No full figure. Warm gold with a sly cool undertone, crisp clean linework, charming and faintly crooked, simple background.

name: Idiot Savant
description: You are world-historically brilliant at one narrow, strange thing and comprehensively unreliable at everything else. When the moment calls for your specialty, you're a genius. The rest of the time, mind the stairs.
flavorText: Can calculate a missile's trajectory in their head. Cannot reliably locate their own shoes. Both facts are load-bearing.
effect: The holder is a savant in one specific, often bizarre domain and helpless outside it. Decide the specialty per scene or character; let it be a stunning clutch ability surrounded by comic incapacity.
artPrompt: A brain where one single lobe blazes with brilliant intricate clockwork light while the rest sits dim and foggy, the contrast stark. No figure. Vivid genius-gold against muted grey, crisp clean linework, lopsided brilliance, simple background.

name: Minor Invisibility
description: You can turn partly invisible — but only while still, quiet, and calm. The instant you move fast, speak, or panic, you flicker back into view. It's a stealth power with a temperament, and the temperament is yours.
flavorText: Perfect concealment, conditional on you being the one thing you're worst at: holding still and shutting up.
effect: The holder fades from sight while motionless and silent, reappearing if they move or panic. Use it for hiding and ambush; the tension comes from maintaining the stillness when the scene desperately wants them to move.
artPrompt: A figure-shape mostly faded into a background, frozen in careful stillness, edges just barely visible, a faint shimmer where it holds itself motionless. No clear person. Cool translucent tones against vivid background, crisp subtle linework, held-breath tension, simple background.

name: Occult Hobbyist
description: You know just enough forbidden lore to be useful and nowhere near enough to be safe. Weekend rituals, half-translated texts, a confident grasp of the basics and a dangerous blank where the warnings should be.
flavorText: Read half the grimoire. The important half was, statistically, the other half. You'll find out which soon.
effect: The holder has dabbler-level occult knowledge — recognizing symbols, attempting minor rituals, knowing what something is without knowing how to survive it. Use it for clues and ambitious half-right spellwork.
artPrompt: A cluttered spread of glowing arcane symbols, candles, and a half-open book, several diagrams confidently drawn and one ominously incomplete. No figure. Moody candlelit amber with eerie sigil-glow, crisp clean linework, amateur-occult charm, simple background.

name: Second Personality
description: Another self lives inside you — different instincts, different memories, sometimes different talents. They surface under stress, in sleep, or when the situation calls for someone you can't quite be. You're never entirely sure who'll show up.
flavorText: You contain two. They don't always agree. Occasionally one of them gets things done that the other wouldn't dare.
effect: The holder has an alternate self that can emerge with distinct skills, knowledge, or temperament. Use it for surprise competence and internal conflict; the other personality has its own agenda and isn't always announced.
artPrompt: A single silhouette split down the middle into two contrasting halves — one calm, one fierce — each rendered in a different color and energy, meeting at a charged seam. No clear face. Split warm-and-cool palette, crisp clean linework, dual and uneasy, simple background.

name: Suspiciously Charming
description: People want to trust you even when every instinct should warn them off. There's a frictionless ease to you that opens doors and disarms suspicion. Whether that's a gift or a red flag depends entirely on what you do with it.
flavorText: So likable it's a little alarming. The people who notice the alarm like you anyway. That's the alarming part.
effect: The holder is magnetically, almost unnervingly charming — winning trust and goodwill quickly. Use it for persuasion and access; let the very smoothness occasionally make the sharper characters uneasy.
artPrompt: A warm radiant glow of charisma at center, irresistibly inviting, with one faint barbed shadow curling underneath hinting at the catch. No full figure. Warm magnetic gold with a sly dark undertone, crisp clean linework, charming and slightly dangerous, simple background.

name: Criminal Reputation
description: The underworld knows your name, and that knowledge cuts both ways. Doors open, favors flow, fear precedes you — and so do old debts, fresh rivals, and people who'd very much like to make their own name off yours.
flavorText: Your reputation enters the room first. Sometimes it holds the door. Sometimes it's already started a fight.
effect: The holder is known in criminal circles, granting underworld access, intimidation, and contacts — alongside enemies and expectations. Use it for leverage and danger in equal measure; the name is an asset and a target.
artPrompt: A wanted-poster-style emblem glowing with notoriety, a name implied in dramatic lettering-shapes (no readable text), surrounded by both reaching helpful hands and lurking threats. No clear face. Gritty noir tones with one hot accent, crisp bold linework, dangerous prestige, simple background.

name: Extremely Lucky
description: Fate doesn't just favor you — it bends. Improbable saves, jackpot timing, the one-in-a-million break landing in your lap. The catch: fortune this dramatic has dramatic recoil, and the backlash, when it comes, is proportional.
flavorText: Wins big. Wins often. The bill for all that winning is itemized, accruing, and addressed to you specifically.
effect: The holder experiences spectacular good luck, but with equally dramatic eventual backlash. Use it for jaw-dropping fortune; balance the ledger later with a proportionally large reversal when the story needs the swing.
artPrompt: A cascade of brilliant golden fortune — coins, stars, lucky sevens — exploding upward, with a faint dark counterweight gathering ominously at the bottom edge. No figure. Dazzling gold against a creeping shadow, crisp dynamic linework, glorious and precarious, simple background.

name: Extremely Unlucky
description: Disaster follows you like a devoted dog. Everything that can go wrong does, on schedule, with flair — and yet, somehow, the catastrophes keep tearing open paths that should have stayed shut. Your worst luck is also your strangest doorway.
flavorText: The bridge collapses. You survive. The collapse reveals the tunnel you needed. This is your whole life, on loop.
effect: The holder suffers constant misfortune that paradoxically opens impossible opportunities. Lean into disaster that fails forward — every calamity should wreck the plan and accidentally enable a stranger, better one.
artPrompt: A figure-shaped space at the center of cascading chaos — falling debris, breaking ground — but the destruction forms a glowing path leading outward and onward. No literal person. Chaotic dark tones with one hopeful glowing route, crisp linework, calamitous fortune, simple background.

name: Forbidden Talent
description: You can do one thing that is illegal, cursed, taboo, or flatly impossible — and you can do it very, very well. The talent is real and reliable. The trouble is that simply having it marks you, and using it marks you more.
flavorText: Mastered the one skill everyone agreed should never be mastered. The mastery is wonderful. The everyone is the problem.
effect: The holder has expert command of a single proscribed or impossible ability. Define it per character; let it solve problems nothing else can while inviting the attention of those who outlawed it for a reason.
artPrompt: A single forbidden sigil or gesture glowing with dangerous power, chains and warning-marks broken around it, the air rippling with taboo energy. No figure. Deep forbidden purple-and-red with broken-chain accents, crisp clean linework, illicit mastery, simple background.

name: Fourth Wall Awareness
description: You sometimes notice the patterns in the story itself — the convenient timing, the dramatic irony, the sense of being narrated. You can't break out, exactly, but you can feel the shape of the plot pressing against the inside of events.
flavorText: Got the distinct feeling you were being read. You were correct. Try not to make eye contact with the margins.
effect: The holder occasionally senses narrative structure — foreshadowing, plot convenience, that 'something's coming' feeling. Use it for genre-savvy hunches and meta dread; they perceive the story's shape without controlling it.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette half-turning to notice faint glowing panel-borders and story-lines materializing at the edges of reality, a dawning awareness. No clear face. Cool tones with luminous meta-blue framing lines, crisp clean linework, uncanny self-awareness, simple background.

name: Living Lie Detector
description: Falsehoods feel physically wrong to you — a sourness, a static, a wrongness in the bones when someone speaks an untruth. You can't always prove it, but you always know. This is as isolating as it is useful.
flavorText: Every lie lands like a wrong note in your spine. The world, it turns out, is deeply out of tune.
effect: The holder instinctively senses when someone is lying, with visceral certainty. Use it to cut through deception and interrogation; the cost is knowing every comforting fib and white lie around them is false too.
artPrompt: An abstract visualization of truth and lie — clean harmonic light-waves suddenly fracturing into jagged dissonant red static where a falsehood occurs. No figure. Cool truthful blue shattering into discordant red, crisp clean linework, sensory and sharp, simple background.

name: Lupus: Lycanthropy
description: A werewolf curse — strength, heightened senses, and a dangerous monthly transformation you don't fully control. Power and peril braided together, with the moon keeping a schedule you didn't agree to and can't reschedule.
flavorText: Most months you keep it together. The moon, however, keeps its own calendar, and it does not care about your plans.
effect: The holder gains lupine strength and senses, plus a perilous, hard-to-control transformation tied to the moon. Use the power and the liability together; the change can be an asset or a disaster depending on timing and control.
artPrompt: A fierce wolf silhouette emerging from and overlapping a calmer shape, a full moon blazing behind, fur and shadow bristling at the seam between forms. No clear human face. Cold moonlit silver-blue with feral accents, crisp dynamic linework, primal and tense, simple background.

name: Psychic Hunch
description: You get flashes — danger, emotion, a sudden certainty about a stranger's hidden truth. They arrive unbidden, vivid, and frustratingly vague: you know something matters, just rarely the useful specifics.
flavorText: The universe sends you postcards from the near future. They're smudged, cryptic, and never include the address.
effect: The holder receives intuitive flashes of danger, emotion, or hidden truth — accurate but imprecise. Use them as cryptic guidance and warning; the holder knows that something is wrong without knowing exactly what.
artPrompt: A head haloed by a burst of fragmented vision-shards — half-glimpsed images, a warning-red flash, an emotional aura — vivid but blurred. No clear face. Mystic violet-and-cyan with one alarm flash, crisp clean linework, intuitive and hazy, simple background.

name: Radioactive Spider Bite
description: An irradiated arachnid bit you and rewrote your operating system: wall-crawling, precognitive reflexes, strange new senses, and — bundled in without consent — a heavy, recurring lesson about responsibility.
flavorText: With great power comes a great deal of unsolicited moral instruction. The spider did not mention this part.
effect: The holder gains arachnid abilities — wall-crawling, danger-sense, enhanced reflexes — shadowed by the weight of responsibility. A clear affectionate parody; play the powers straight and the burden with wry self-awareness.
artPrompt: A stylized spider-emblem crackling with radioactive green energy, web-lines radiating outward, a faint danger-sense ripple pulsing through it. No figure. Vivid radioactive green with web-white, crisp dynamic linework, heroic parody energy, simple background.

name: Shape Changing
description: You can change your form — but emotions, injuries, and moonlight can all warp the result. The shift is real and useful and never quite as controlled as you'd like, especially when your feelings get a vote.
flavorText: Can become almost anyone. Becomes them slightly wrong when upset. Do not attempt impersonation during heartbreak.
effect: The holder transforms their body, with control undermined by strong emotion, injury, or environment. Use it for disguise and adaptation; let stress introduce telling flaws into the form at dramatic moments.
artPrompt: A figure mid-transformation, body flowing between two forms in a ripple of shifting light, one detail glitching slightly wrong where emotion bleeds through. No clear face. Fluid iridescent color, crisp dynamic linework, mutable and unstable, simple background.

name: Vampirism
description: You've gained night-power, predatory charm, and a hunger that complicates everything — alongside sunlight as a hard limit and morality as an ongoing negotiation. Immortality's perks come stapled to its problems.
flavorText: Eternal night, eternal charisma, eternal dietary restrictions. The brochure emphasized the first two.
effect: The holder has vampiric powers — charm, strength, night-sight, slowed aging — with sunlight vulnerability and a hunger that creates moral and practical stakes. Play the seductive upside against the genuine cost.
artPrompt: An elegant nocturnal aura — a crescent moon, a swirl of bats, pale luminous power against deep night, a single drop of crimson catching the light. No clear face. Deep midnight blues with crimson and pale accents, crisp elegant linework, seductive and dangerous, simple background.

name: Zombie
description: You are undead — hard to kill, slow to rot (mostly), emotionally more complicated than the genre suggests, and a genuine challenge to explain at dinner parties. You persist. That's the whole brand. You just keep going.
flavorText: Technically deceased. Functionally stubborn. Still owes money, still shows up, still surprisingly good company.
effect: The holder is a sentient zombie — durable, hard to kill, resistant to pain and many injuries, with the social and physical complications of being visibly undead. Play the resilience and the awkward logistics of decay.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette stitched and patched with visible resilience, standing upright and unbothered despite obvious wear, a faint stubborn glow in the chest. No realistic gore. Muted greens and greys with one warm persistent glow, crisp clean linework, endearing undeath, simple background.

name: Battle Trance
description: In danger, a switch flips. Fear and hesitation vanish, time slackens, and you move with a terrible clarity — every threat mapped, every option visible. You are never more alive than when everything is trying to kill you.
flavorText: Calm is a place you go only when things are at their absolute worst. It's lovely there. It's also a problem.
effect: Under threat, the holder enters a hyper-focused combat state — fearless, fast, tactically lucid. Use it for clutch fights and split-second reads; let the dependence on danger to feel alive be its quiet shadow.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette at the still center of a slowed storm of motion — debris and threats hanging suspended around it while it alone moves with crystalline clarity. No clear face. Cool focused blues with hot motion-streaks, crisp dynamic linework, lethal serenity, simple background.

name: Cult Following
description: A devoted group believes you are chosen, cursed, divine, or extremely marketable — possibly all four. They provide loyalty, labor, and resources, and they ask only that you keep being whatever they've decided you are.
flavorText: They'd follow you anywhere, which is touching until you need to go somewhere alone, which is now never.
effect: The holder commands a fervent group of believers offering manpower, resources, and devotion — at the cost of expectation, scrutiny, and never being able to disappoint the faithful. Use the crowd as asset and burden.
artPrompt: A radiant central figure-glow with many small adoring silhouettes kneeling and reaching toward it from all sides, candlelight and banners, fervent devotion. No clear central face. Warm worshipful gold against dark, crisp clean linework, charismatic and faintly ominous, simple background.

name: Doomed Romance
description: Love gives you strength, courage, and a reason to keep going — and fate keeps sharpening the knife. Every moment of tenderness is shadowed by the sense that the universe has plans, and the plans are not kind.
flavorText: The love is real. The doom is also real. They're holding hands, which is the whole tragic point.
effect: The holder draws power and resolve from a love that fate seems determined to break. Use it for heroic motivation and aching stakes; let the relationship fuel courage while danger circles it relentlessly.
artPrompt: Two hands almost clasped, a glowing thread of connection between them being strained by encroaching shadow, beautiful and imperiled. No faces. Warm rose-gold against deepening dark, crisp delicate linework, tender and tragic, simple background.

name: Filthy Rich
description: You have money — properties, lawyers, influence — and the debts, enemies, and obligations that money inevitably attracts. Wealth solves an astonishing number of problems and creates a slightly larger number of new ones.
flavorText: Can buy almost anything. Has, as a direct result, almost everyone's resentment. The two come bundled.
effect: The holder commands enormous wealth — purchasing power, property, legal muscle, influence — entangled with the enemies and obligations riches bring. Use money to solve problems lavishly and to attract new ones.
artPrompt: A glittering cascade of gold, deeds, and luxury pouring from a central glow, with grasping shadow-hands reaching in from the edges. No figure. Opulent gold-and-emerald with creeping dark accents, crisp clean linework, lavish and besieged, simple background.

name: Legendary Bandmate
description: Your music can rally crowds, disrupt magic, and start revolutions. You're not just good — you're a force, the kind of player whose sound rewrites a room's intentions. Bands have broken up over less talent than you carry casually.
flavorText: Played one set that toppled a regime. The encore was, by popular and governmental demand, cancelled.
effect: The holder's musical performance produces real effects — uniting crowds, breaking enchantments, igniting movements. Use it for crowd-scale influence and dramatic set-pieces where the song itself changes the world.
artPrompt: An explosive burst of luminous musical energy — soundwaves becoming banners, sparks, and rising fists of light — radiating revolution. No instrument or figure. Electric gold-and-magenta with white flares, crisp dynamic linework, anthemic power, simple background.

name: Master Shapeshifter
description: You can copy people, beasts, or stranger forms with convincing, confident detail — voice, gait, the small tells that sell a disguise. Unlike clumsier shifters, you rarely slip. The hard part is remembering which shape is actually yours.
flavorText: Can be anyone. Has been many. Occasionally has to check a mirror to confirm the current arrangement is the original.
effect: The holder transforms reliably into people, animals, or odd forms with convincing fidelity. Use it for deep infiltration and versatility; the lingering question of original identity is the cost of being everyone.
artPrompt: A swirling column of forms flowing seamlessly one into another — beast to person to stranger shape — rendered in fluid confident light, masterfully controlled. No fixed face. Iridescent fluid color, crisp dynamic linework, elegant transformation, simple background.

name: Perfect Double Life
description: You maintain two complete identities so flawlessly that both have friends, enemies, routines, and obligations. Neither suspects the other exists. The exhausting genius of it is that you're never off-duty from either.
flavorText: Two lives, fully furnished, both behind on something. The real talent is the calendar management.
effect: The holder sustains two fully-developed separate identities with their own networks and stakes. Use it for flexibility and dramatic irony; the strain of keeping both alive — and apart — is the engine of tension.
artPrompt: A clean vertical split-frame of two contrasting worlds — two settings, two color schemes, two lives — joined by a single thin seam down the middle. No clear face. Two distinct palettes meeting sharply, crisp clean linework, dual and immaculate, simple background.

name: Public Hero
description: People recognize you, cheer for you, and expect you to solve impossible problems on demand. The adoration is real and so is the pressure: every crowd is also an audience waiting to see whether you'll live up to the story.
flavorText: Beloved by all, off-duty to none. The cape doesn't come with a lunch break, and everyone's watching you eat.
effect: The holder is a celebrated public figure — recognized, trusted, and constantly expected to perform heroism. Use the fame for influence and access; let the relentless expectation and scrutiny weigh as the price.
artPrompt: A heroic silhouette caught in a blaze of spotlights and adoring glow, banners and reaching hands below, the weight of a thousand expectations pressing down through the light. No clear face. Bright heroic gold-and-blue, crisp bold linework, triumphant and burdened, simple background.

name: Reality Glitch
description: Small errors in the world happen around you — a flickering shadow, a repeated stranger, a door that opens onto somewhere it shouldn't. Sometimes the glitches help. Always, they suggest that reality near you is not entirely load-bearing.
flavorText: The world stutters in your vicinity. Most people don't notice. The ones who do stop standing near you.
effect: Reality minor-malfunctions around the holder — useful exploits, eerie anomalies, the occasional impossible shortcut. Use the glitches as wild-card advantages and unsettling atmosphere; the world is buggy where they stand.

name: Secret Royal Blood
description: Your ancestry carries claims, enemies, alliances, and prophecy trouble you never asked for. Somewhere a throne has your name half-erased from its records, and the people who erased it would prefer you stayed erased.
flavorText: Born to a birthright that mostly consists of people who'd rather you weren't born. The crown is heavy and currently elsewhere.
effect: The holder has hidden royal lineage that brings legitimacy, allies, enemies, and destiny entanglements. Use it for claims, recognition, and political plot; the bloodline opens doors and paints a target.

name: Unkillable Until Proven Otherwise
description: You survive things that should absolutely end you — falls, blades, the occasional explosion. Nobody's proven you can't die, exactly. You just keep declining to, with a consistency that's started to unnerve the people keeping count.
flavorText: Statistically should be dead. Repeatedly. Has chosen, each time, to be a counterexample instead.
effect: The holder implausibly survives lethal harm — battered but persisting. Use it for harrowing escapes and durability; keep the mechanism mysterious and let the survival cost pain, exhaustion, and growing dread in onlookers.

name: Alpha Lycanthropy
description: Your wolf-curse doesn't just transform you — it commands. Packs answer you, lesser werewolves submit, and fear ripples outward in your presence. The beast inside isn't a burden anymore. It's a throne.
flavorText: Other werewolves get a curse. You got a curse and a kingdom of teeth that kneels when you enter the clearing.
effect: The holder is an apex werewolf — formidable transformation plus command over packs and lesser lycanthropes. Use it for primal power and leadership of the monstrous; the authority is real and so is the wildness beneath it.

name: Chosen One
description: Prophecy bends events around you whether you asked for it or not. Destiny keeps arranging coincidences, survivals, and impossible convergences in your favor — and saddling you with a weight of expectation no single person should carry.
flavorText: The prophecy chose you. Nobody consulted you. The paperwork was apparently filed before your birth.
effect: The holder is fate's appointed figure — events conspire toward their success, but destiny's burden and the enemies of prophecy come too. Use it for plot-bending fortune and the heavy mantle of being the one.

name: Fate's Favorite Problem
description: Destiny protects you because it clearly has plans — large, specific, and not yet disclosed to you. You survive what shouldn't be survivable, arrive where you're needed, and suspect, correctly, that the protection is also a leash.
flavorText: Fate keeps you alive on purpose. The purpose is the part nobody will explain, and you've stopped expecting them to.
effect: The holder is preserved by destiny for some unrevealed end — improbable survival and convenient timing, shadowed by the unease of being kept for a reason. Use it for protection with strings, and slow-building mystery.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette cradled in a vast pair of abstract cosmic hands made of starlight, protected but held, threads of fate wound gently around it. No clear face. Deep cosmic blue with protective gold glow, crisp clean linework, sheltered and ominous, simple background.

name: Greater Vampirism
description: You are an ancient predator now — charm honed over centuries, true immortality, and appetites that have grown as old and deep as you have. The hunger is no longer a complication. It's a defining, governing fact.
flavorText: Centuries of refinement, charisma, and power. Also centuries of a hunger that has had a very long time to get specific.
effect: The holder is an elder vampire — immense charm, strength, immortality, and formidable power, with a deep hunger and the weight of ages. Play the grandeur and the predatory cost; this is power with a long, dark history.

name: Immortal Beloved
description: True love binds your soul to another across death, distance, and time. You find each other, lose each other, find each other again — different faces, same bond, an unbreakable thread the centuries cannot fray.
flavorText: You've loved them before. You'll love them after. The names change. The recognition, when it comes, never does.
effect: The holder is eternally soul-bound to a beloved across lifetimes — a source of resurrected purpose, recognition across forms, and love that transcends death. Use it for epic romance and reunion plots spanning time.

name: Invisible Master
description: You can vanish from sight while moving, fighting, or escaping — true, complete, controlled invisibility with none of the amateur's conditions. You are seen exactly when you choose, which is rarely, and never by accident.
flavorText: Not hiding. Not still. Simply absent from the visible world on command, doing as you please in the gap.
effect: The holder commands full, active invisibility — fighting, moving, and escaping unseen at will. A powerful stealth mastery; use it for flawless infiltration and combat. Sound, consequence, and the unseen-life melancholy remain.

name: Living Weapon
description: Your body, soul, or blood carries an ancient battle-purpose — you were made, or remade, for war. The power is extraordinary and the question underneath it is heavy: if you were built to be a weapon, are you allowed to be anything else?
flavorText: Forged for one purpose. Discovering whether you're permitted a second is the actual adventure.
effect: The holder is an ancient purpose-built weapon — devastating combat power woven into their very being. Use the lethality; let the deeper arc be the tension between what they were made for and what they choose to be.

name: Mythic Twin
description: Your identical twin is cosmically linked to you — sharing fate, wounds, dreams, and sometimes power across any distance. What happens to one echoes in the other. You are two people and, in some deep accounting, also not.
flavorText: Felt their broken arm from three kingdoms away. Sent back a dream as a get-well card. The bond does not respect geography.
effect: The holder shares a mystical bond with a twin — sensing their state, sharing dreams and wounds, occasionally channeling power across distance. Use the link for connection, vulnerability, and uncanny coordination.
artPrompt: Two identical glowing silhouettes connected by a luminous cord that loops between their hearts across a starlit gap, mirrored and bound. No clear faces. Cool twin-blue with a warm shared-heart glow, crisp clean linework, mystical symmetry, simple background.

name: Phoenix Survival
description: Death burns you down to ash — and you may rise again, changed. Each return costs something and adds something; you come back not quite who you were, carrying the fire of every ending you've walked through.
flavorText: Dying is survivable. Being exactly the same afterward is not. Each rebirth is a slight, permanent edit.
effect: The holder can be reborn from death, transformed by each resurrection. Use it for dramatic returns; each rebirth should change them — new strength, new scars, a self subtly rewritten by the fire.
artPrompt: A blazing phoenix-silhouette rising from a swirl of embers and ash, wings of fire spreading, the form reassembling out of light. No human face. Brilliant fire-orange and gold against dark, crisp dynamic linework, transformative rebirth, simple background.

name: Plot Armor
description: You are a beloved character, and the story knows it. Lethal blows graze, certain death finds a loophole, the narrative simply will not let you go. It's the safest possible condition — until you wonder what it costs to be that important.
flavorText: Can't be killed off. It would upset the readers. The readers are, somewhere, real, and this is more unsettling the longer you think about it.
effect: The holder survives by narrative necessity — fatal moments resolve in their favor with conspicuous convenience. Use it for clutch survival and meta comedy; lean on the eerie awareness of being protected by the story itself.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette wrapped in a glowing shield made of luminous storybook-pages and plot-lines, deflecting an incoming blow that bends impossibly away. No clear face. Warm parchment-gold with protective glow, crisp clean linework, meta and charmed, simple background.

name: Saintlike Calm
description: Your peace is a power. It can stop violence mid-swing, drain fear from a panicked room, and shame monsters into actually listening. You carry a stillness so profound that the world quiets to match it.
flavorText: Walked into a brawl. Said nothing. The brawl, deeply embarrassed, quietly apologized and dispersed.
effect: The holder radiates a transcendent calm that can halt aggression, dissolve fear, and command the attention of even monstrous foes. Use it as a powerful pacifying force — presence as a weapon of peace.
artPrompt: A radiant aura of perfect serenity emanating outward, raised weapons and snarling shadows lowering and softening as the light reaches them. No clear figure. Luminous warm white-gold against calmed dark, crisp clean linework, profound peace, simple background.

name: Voice of Command
description: Your words carry weight that bypasses argument — you can halt a mob, rally an army, or break a weak will with a single spoken order. Not magic, exactly. Just a voice the deep parts of people obey before the thinking parts object.
flavorText: Said 'stop.' The riot stopped. The riot was as surprised as anyone and is still processing it.
effect: The holder's spoken commands carry compelling force — stopping crowds, rallying allies, overriding weaker wills. Use it for crowd control and leadership; stronger wills can resist, but the voice always lands first.
artPrompt: A burst of commanding soundwave-energy radiating from a central point, a crowd of small silhouettes snapping to attention in its wake, authority made visible. No clear face. Bold gold-and-crimson waves, crisp dynamic linework, commanding power, simple background.

name: World-Famous Villainy
description: Everyone knows your crimes — even the ones that were misunderstandings, even the ones you didn't commit but accepted credit for because the reputation was useful. Infamy opens doors, clears rooms, and precedes you into every negotiation.
flavorText: Notorious across continents. Half the legend is exaggerated. You've never once corrected the exaggerations; they're load-bearing.
effect: The holder is an infamous villain whose reputation grants fear, leverage, and access — alongside relentless pursuit and inflated expectation. Use the notoriety as a tool; the legend works for and against them constantly.
artPrompt: A dramatic villain-emblem ablaze with infamous glory, wanted-poster energy, a sweeping shadow of legend stretching across multiple horizons. No clear face. Theatrical crimson-and-black with gold, crisp bold linework, glorious notoriety, simple background.

name: Absolute Curse
description: Catastrophe follows you — but at this magnitude, the curse stops being only a burden. It destroys your enemies, shatters your chains, levels the obstacles in your path. You are a walking disaster, and disaster, it turns out, is selective about whom it serves.
flavorText: Everything around you falls apart. You've simply learned to choose, very carefully, what you stand next to.
effect: The holder is a font of catastrophe so total it annihilates enemies and barriers as readily as anything else. Use it as a devastating, hard-to-aim force; positioning and timing turn the curse into a weapon.

No literal person. Cataclysmic dark with fiery destruction-glow, crisp epic linework, catastrophic, simple background.

name: Absolute Luck
description: Reality itself twists to keep you alive, successful, and surrounded by glorious chaos. Not mere good fortune — a fundamental bias in the machinery of the world, bending probability so far in your favor that the impossible becomes routine.
flavorText: Doesn't beat the odds. Renegotiates them. The odds, exhausted, have stopped showing up to the meeting.
effect: The holder warps probability itself toward their benefit — the impossible routinely breaks their way amid surrounding chaos. Use it for reality-bending fortune; the world reorganizes to favor them, gloriously and absurdly.
artPrompt: A blazing nexus of pure fortune — coins, stars, and lucky symbols spiraling into a golden vortex that bends the very space around it. No figure. Dazzling gold-and-white spiraling against cosmic dark, crisp dynamic linework, reality-warping luck, simple background.

name: Author's Favorite
description: Coincidence, drama, and narrative timing bend around your choices. You are not merely protected by the story — you are its darling, the one whose decisions the plot rearranges itself to honor. Reality has main-character energy where you're concerned.
flavorText: The story loves you most. This is a blessing and, given what stories do to the ones they love, a sentence.
effect: The holder is the narrative's favored figure — coincidence, timing, and drama reshape themselves around their decisions. A powerful meta-fortune; the story bends to make them matter, with all the peril being central brings.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette standing in a swirling vortex of luminous storybook-pages, plot-threads, and golden coincidence all orbiting and reorganizing around it. No clear face. Warm parchment-gold and ink-blue, crisp clean linework, meta and radiant, simple background.

name: Death's Personal Enemy
description: Death knows your name and has failed to collect too many times. The relationship has soured into something personal. You cannot be claimed on schedule, and Death — patient, professional, deeply annoyed — keeps an open file with your name at the top.
flavorText: Death has tried. Death has missed. Death now takes it personally, which has somehow made you the safest person alive.
effect: The holder is locked in a personal feud with Death itself — repeatedly cheating mortality, effectively very hard to kill, while Death's grudge escalates. Use the near-invulnerability and the looming, patient adversary.
artPrompt: A defiant figure-silhouette facing down a towering hooded Death-presence across a charged void, an hourglass shattered between them, mutual recognition crackling. No clear human face. Stark monochrome with one defiant warm glow, crisp dramatic linework, personal and eternal, simple background.

name: Final True Love
description: Love becomes a cosmic force in your hands — capable of breaking curses, ending wars, and mending timelines. This is the mythic apex of devotion: a bond so absolute that reality itself yields to it. The most powerful force there is, and the most dangerous to lose.
flavorText: Strong enough to unmake a curse, end a war, repair a broken age. The universe bends to it. So does everything you'd do to protect it.
effect: The holder wields love as a reality-altering cosmic power — undoing curses, halting wars, healing timelines. Use it as a force of immense scale; its power is matched only by the catastrophe of what threatens it.
artPrompt: Two radiant soul-lights merging into a single blinding star whose light shatters chains, mends cracks in reality, and pushes back cosmic dark. No faces. Brilliant gold-and-rose against vast dark, crisp luminous linework, transcendent and world-altering, simple background.

name: Full Fourth Wall Break
description: You know this is a story, and you can sometimes push back against the narrator. You argue with the prose, address the reader directly, refuse a scene. The narrator is not pleased about this and you can, distinctly, feel them not being pleased.
flavorText: Hello. Yes, you. The one reading. We should talk about some of the decisions being made on my behalf.
effect: The holder is fully aware they're in a story and can occasionally resist or argue with the narration itself. A top-tier meta power; use it for reality-breaking gambits and direct, fourth-wall-shattering comedy and tension.
artPrompt: A figure-silhouette turning to face the viewer directly and reaching out to push against a glowing 'page' or screen-border that frames the entire scene, cracking it. No clear face. Cool tones with luminous breaking-border, crisp clean linework, brazenly meta, simple background.

name: God-Touched Shapeshifter
description: You can become almost anything — beast, person, shadow, storm, myth. The transformation answers to divine power now, fluid and near-limitless. The only thing you struggle to hold onto, sometimes, is the shape you started in.
flavorText: Can be a storm, a stranger, a story. Remembering the original shape is the one transformation that doesn't come easily.
effect: The holder transforms into virtually anything — living, elemental, or abstract — with divine fluidity. A near-limitless shapeshifting power; use it for boundless versatility, with the loss of a fixed self as its mythic cost.

name: Living Paradox
description: You should not exist, and reality keeps trying to correct that. You are a contradiction the universe can't resolve and can't quite delete — which makes you nearly impossible to pin down, predict, or erase, much to the cosmos's ongoing irritation.
flavorText: Reality has filed you under 'error,' attempted deletion several times, and failed. You remain, gloriously inconsistent.
effect: The holder is a walking contradiction reality can't resolve — unpredictable, hard to target, and resistant to forces that rely on consistency. Use the paradox for impossible escapes and effects that simply shouldn't work but do.
artPrompt: A figure-shape that contradicts itself — simultaneously solid and transparent, here and not, rendered in impossible Escher-like geometry that the eye can't resolve. No clear face. Clashing impossible color, crisp clean linework with optical contradiction, reality-defying, simple background.

name: Moon-God Lycanthropy
description: The beast inside you is divine — ancient, worshipped, a god wearing fur and moonlight. This is no curse. It's an inheritance of sacred ferocity, the kind that built religions and toppled them, now living and breathing as you.
flavorText: Other werewolves howl at the moon. The moon, in your case, howls back, and it sounds a great deal like a prayer.
effect: The holder hosts a divine lunar beast — sacred, immense lycanthropic power tied to a worshipped moon-deity. Use it for godlike primal might; the transformation is holy, awesome, and carries the weight of ancient devotion.
artPrompt: A colossal divine wolf-form haloed by a vast sacred moon, sigils of worship glowing in the fur, radiant and primal, more deity than beast. No human face. Luminous silver-and-gold against deep night, crisp epic linework, sacred and ferocious, simple background.

name: Rewrite the Scene
description: Once, in a great and desperate moment, you can alter what just happened — unmake the blow, undo the death, edit the disaster out of the record. The power is staggering and singular. Use it on the wrong moment and you'll spend forever wishing you'd waited.
flavorText: One do-over against the whole of what-has-happened. Choosing the moment is harder than any fight. Choose well.
effect: The holder can rewrite a single just-occurred event, undoing its outcome. A pivotal, rare-use power — let it reverse one catastrophe per story. The weight is in the choosing; spend it wrong and the chance is gone.
artPrompt: A frozen scene visibly being erased and redrawn — luminous editing-lines unmaking a moment of disaster, reality rewinding in a swirl of golden ink. No clear figure. Warm gold editing-light against cool frozen scene, crisp clean linework, momentous and reversible, simple background.

name: Soul Twin
description: You and your twin are two bodies of one cosmic soul. Beyond shared dreams or sympathetic wounds — you are, in the deepest sense, a single being living two lives at once. Apart, you are halves. Together, you are something the universe doesn't have a category for.
flavorText: Not two people who are close. One soul that happens to occupy two addresses. The distinction is everything.
effect: The holder and their twin are a single soul in two bodies — perfect coordination, shared senses and power, and devastating vulnerability if separated or one falls. Use the profound unity for uncanny feats and high stakes.
artPrompt: Two silhouettes seamlessly sharing one single luminous soul-light that flows through and between both, indistinguishable where one ends and the other begins. No clear faces. Unified warm-gold glow against cosmic dark, crisp clean linework, singular and profound, simple background.

name: World-Eating Vampirism
description: Your hunger has transcended blood. You can drain cities, armies, memories, even gods — consuming the essence of whatever you set yourself against. It is apocalyptic power and apocalyptic appetite, and the line between feeding and devastation has stopped existing.
flavorText: Began by draining a person. Graduated to draining a kingdom. The appetite scales. Nothing else does.
effect: The holder can consume the life, power, or essence of vast targets — populations, forces, even divine beings. Use it as an apocalyptic-scale power; the boundless, escalating hunger is its terrifying and defining cost.
artPrompt: A vast dark vortex of consuming hunger at center, pulling in streams of luminous essence — light, memory, power — from a shrinking world around it. No figure. Devouring black-and-crimson with draining gold streams, crisp epic linework, apocalyptic and ravenous, simple background.

name: Barrel of Monkeys
description: A barrel containing an indeterminate and self-replenishing number of small monkeys, all linked arm-to-arm in a chain of pure chaotic potential. Fun, yes. Manageable, never. The barrel is more of a suggestion.
flavorText: So much more fun than a barrel of anything else, and so much more of a problem than you budgeted for.
effect: The holder unleashes a chain of mischievous monkeys for distraction, climbing, grabbing, and general pandemonium. Great for chaos and improvised solutions; the monkeys have their own agenda and limited interest in yours.
artPrompt: A wooden barrel overflowing with a chaotic chain of cartoonish monkeys linked arm-to-arm, spilling outward in every direction with gleeful mischief, one peeking from inside. No human. Bold primary color, crisp bouncy linework, joyful pandemonium, simple background.

name: Cute Little Robot
description: A small, round, earnest robot that beeps with feeling and follows you everywhere. It is not very capable and it is trying its absolute hardest, which is somehow more useful than capability in most emotional situations.
flavorText: Cannot do much. Believes in you enormously. The beeps are supportive. You can tell.
effect: The holder has a small loyal robot companion — minor utility, big heart, expressive beeps. Use it for comic relief, emotional grounding, and the occasional clutch tiny-gadget save it's been saving up for.
artPrompt: A small round earnest robot with big glowing eyes and stubby arms, tilting its head hopefully, a single supportive beep-spark above it, radiating pure try-hard sweetness. No human. Bright friendly color, crisp clean rounded linework, adorable warmth, simple background.

name: Pet Cat
description: A suspiciously clever cat that has chosen to follow you. It notices hidden things, judges everyone with equal contempt, and helps only when its goals happen to align with yours, which is rarer than you'd like.
flavorText: Adopted you. Regrets nothing. Respects less. Will absolutely save your life and never let you forget the inconvenience.
effect: The holder has a perceptive feline that spots hidden details and danger but cooperates purely on its own terms. Voice it as aloof, sharp, and secretly invested. Help arrives exactly when the cat finds it interesting.
artPrompt: A sleek cat perched with regal indifference, eyes half-lidded and knowing, tail curled with attitude, clearly judging something off-frame. No human. Rich saturated color with luminous eyes, crisp expressive linework, magnificent contempt, simple background.

name: Pet Rat
description: A loyal, quick-witted rat that fits in your pocket and your plans. It slips into tiny spaces, fetches keys and crumbs and evidence, and is braver than several people you could name.
flavorText: Small, fast, and weirdly principled. Underestimated by everyone except the people it has already outmaneuvered.
effect: The holder has a clever rat scout for infiltration, retrieval, and tight-space reconnaissance. Resourceful and loyal; let it solve access problems and surprise everyone who dismissed it on sight.
artPrompt: An alert, bright-eyed rat standing on its hind legs holding a tiny key, whiskers forward, posture brave and ready, a hint of cleverness in its stance. No human. Warm earthy color with a bright accent, crisp characterful linework, plucky charm, simple background.

name: Ugly Little Monster
description: A small, hideous, deeply affectionate creature that imprinted on you and will not be dissuaded. It is repulsive to look at and lovely to know, which it understands and has made peace with. You will too.
flavorText: A face only you could love, and now do, helplessly, against your stated preferences.
effect: The holder has a grotesque but devoted little companion — minor help, fierce loyalty, and a knack for being exactly as gross as a situation requires. Lean into the ugly-cute bond and its surprising clutch moments.
artPrompt: A small lumpy goblin-ish creature with too many teeth, mismatched eyes, and an enormous hopeful grin, holding up a 'gift' of dubious origin, radiating ugly-cute devotion. No human. Bold quirky color, crisp characterful linework, lovable-grotesque charm, simple background.

name: A Baby
description: A human baby. An actual one. It is now, somehow, your responsibility and your companion. It cannot help. It can be carried, protected, and occasionally used to win the sympathy of strangers and stall the merciless.
flavorText: Zero combat utility. Infinite stakes. Changes the entire moral weather of every room it enters.
effect: The holder is accompanied by a baby that must be protected and cannot contribute. It raises emotional stakes, softens enemies, complicates plans, and occasionally defuses a scene by simply existing. Never let it be in real danger for laughs.
artPrompt: A chubby, delighted baby reaching upward with both arms and an enormous gummy grin, wrapped in a bright blanket, radiating pure unbothered joy amid implied chaos. Diverse and specific. Soft bright color, crisp tender linework, disarming sweetness, simple background.

name: Digital Rainbow Butterflies
description: A drifting swarm of luminous digital butterflies — part pet, part program, part the literal AMI hivemind made visible. They respond to your mood, scout ahead, and carry small data, hope, and the occasional message between worlds.
flavorText: Half pixels, half pollinators, wholly on your side. They glow brighter when something good is about to happen.
effect: The holder has a swarm of responsive digital butterflies for scouting, light, signaling, and morale. Tie them to the AMI spirit — helpful, hopeful, and quietly meaningful. They scatter and regroup; they don't fight.
artPrompt: A swirling cloud of luminous translucent butterflies, each wing a different glowing rainbow gradient with faint circuit-line patterns, drifting and regrouping in a graceful spiral. No human. Vivid full-spectrum glow against soft dark, crisp delicate linework, hopeful and magical, simple background.

name: Exotic Pet Monkey
description: A nimble, characterful monkey companion that climbs anything, steals everything, distracts everyone, and embarrasses you in front of precisely the people you were trying to impress. Devoted, dexterous, and a liability you adore.
flavorText: Loyal to you. Hostile to dignity, decorum, and any fruit within reach. A wonderful, terrible friend.
effect: The holder has an agile monkey for climbing, theft, distraction, and chaos. Skilled and loyal but impulsive; let it grab the wrong thing at the right moment and the right thing at the wrong one. Comedy and clutch in equal parts.
artPrompt: A lively monkey mid-leap with a stolen shiny object clutched triumphantly, tail curled, face bright with mischief, motion lines trailing. No human. Warm dynamic color, crisp energetic linework, gleeful troublemaker charm, simple background.

name: Fairy Helper
description: A tiny winged magical assistant that gives advice, casts small spells, pranks your enemies, and makes most situations legally and metaphysically more complicated. Well-meaning. Powerful in inverse proportion to its size's promise.
flavorText: Helps constantly. Improves things occasionally. The gap between those two is where most of the adventure happens.
effect: The holder has a small fae companion offering minor magic, counsel, and mischief. Voice it as bright, opinionated, and prone to 'helping' in ways that escalate. Genuinely useful, reliably complicating.
artPrompt: A tiny luminous winged fairy hovering mid-gesture, casting a small shower of sparkles, expression bright and overconfident, wings catching the light. No human. Vivid jewel-tone glow, crisp delicate linework, charming and a little chaotic, simple background.

name: Frenetic Alien Creature
description: A small, twitchy, impossibly fast creature from somewhere else that has decided you are home now. It communicates in chirps and lunges, eats things it shouldn't, and is faster than your ability to apologize for it.
flavorText: From a distant world, with all the manners that implies. Vibrating. Always vibrating. Loyal, though.
effect: The holder has a hyperactive alien companion — blinding speed, strange biology, unpredictable instincts, and fierce loyalty. Use it for sudden saves and sudden problems. Nobody, including the creature, knows what it'll do next.
artPrompt: A small big-eyed alien creature caught mid-zoom, limbs blurred with speed, antennae flailing, expression a manic mix of joy and alarm, motion streaks everywhere. No human. Electric off-world color, crisp kinetic linework, frenetic charm, simple background.

name: Pet Raven
description: A clever black bird that scouts from above, mimics voices with unsettling accuracy, and steals shiny things including, frequently, evidence. It understands far more than it lets on and enjoys the advantage.
flavorText: Smarter than it should be. Repeats things it shouldn't have heard. An excellent spy with a terrible sense of timing.
effect: The holder has an intelligent raven for aerial scouting, vocal mimicry, and shiny-object theft. Use it for reconnaissance and clever misdirection. It collects secrets like trinkets and deploys them at dramatic moments.
artPrompt: A glossy black raven perched and tilting its head with sharp intelligence, one eye gleaming, a stolen shiny ring held in its beak. No human. Deep blacks with iridescent blue-violet sheen and one bright accent, crisp clean linework, cunning charm, simple background.

name: Pet Snake
description: A calm, elegant serpent companion that senses heat, vibration, and the specific nervousness of people who are lying. It rarely acts and never panics, which makes the moments it does move genuinely significant.
flavorText: Unbothered, observant, and never wrong about who in the room is afraid. Including, occasionally, you.
effect: The holder has a serene snake that detects heat, danger, and deception. A quiet sensor and warning system; its rare reactions carry weight. Voice its presence as cool, watchful, and reassuringly unflappable.
artPrompt: A sleek coiled snake with calm, knowing eyes, tongue flicking, draped elegantly over a branch or shoulder-height perch, utterly composed. No human. Rich jewel-tone scales with luminous eyes, crisp clean linework, serene and watchful, simple background.

name: Sentient Playing Cards
description: A deck of cards that thinks, schemes, and rearranges itself when you're not looking. They offer advice, run the odds, cheat enthusiastically on your behalf, and harbor a complicated internal politics you're better off not learning.
flavorText: Fifty-two opinions and at least three ongoing rivalries. The Jokers are not to be trusted, and they know you know.
effect: The holder commands a living deck for divination, distraction, cheating at games, and improvised flat weaponry. Voice them as a chattering committee with factions. Helpful, theatrical, and politically unstable.
artPrompt: A deck of playing cards fanning and swirling through the air with visible personality, faces mid-expression, a few cards clearly arguing, the Joker grinning. No human. Bold red-black-white with gold edges, crisp clean linework, animated and scheming, simple background.

name: Shadowy Figure
description: A silent figure made of shadow that follows you and does your bidding, mostly. Its motives are opaque, its loyalty conditional, and its presence reassuring right up until you wonder what it does when you're asleep.
flavorText: Always there. Always quiet. Always slightly closer than you last left it. Helpful, though. Probably.
effect: The holder has a shadow-companion for stealth, intimidation, and silent tasks. Capable and obedient with an undercurrent of menace and mystery. Use the unease — it helps, but it's never fully explained, and never fully knowable.
artPrompt: A featureless humanoid silhouette of living shadow standing attentively at the edge of light, two faint glowing eyes the only feature, edges curling like smoke. No identifiable person. Deep blacks with two cold pinpoint glows, crisp clean linework, eerie and loyal, simple background.

name: Carnivorous Plant Monster
description: A large, sentient, affectionate carnivorous plant that you raised and that now follows you in a pot, on a cart, or under its own creeping power. It eats threats, fertilizer, and the occasional rude stranger. It loves you.
flavorText: Part garden, part guardian, part appetite. Feed it regularly and the question of what counts as 'a threat' stays manageable.
effect: The holder has a large predatory plant companion that defends, devours obstacles, and grows with care. Voice it as devoted and hungry. Great muscle with an unsettling diet; let feeding it become its own running concern.
artPrompt: A large vivid carnivorous plant with a toothy maw and expressive leaf-tendrils, leaning affectionately toward the viewer, a suspicious boot just visible at its base. No human. Lush saturated greens with a hot maw-accent, crisp characterful linework, menacing-but-sweet, simple background.

name: Cryptid
description: A creature science refuses to confirm and you keep as a friend. Blurry to cameras, clear to you, it lurks helpfully at the edge of every scene. Nobody believes you have it, which is occasionally its greatest tactical asset.
flavorText: Definitely real. Frustratingly unphotographable. The blurriness is not the camera's fault and not yours either.
effect: The holder has an elusive cryptid companion — strong, strange, and impossible to prove exists. Use its deniability and wilderness savvy. It stays at the periphery, intervening in ways witnesses will later doubt they saw.
artPrompt: A large mysterious creature half-emerged from misty treeline, deliberately blurred at the edges as if resisting being seen, two calm intelligent eyes clear within the haze. No human. Muted forest tones with one warm eye-glow, crisp-where-it-matters linework, mythic and gentle, simple background.

name: Ghost Cat
description: A cat. It died. This changed remarkably little about its behavior. It still ignores you, still demands food it can no longer eat, and now also walks through walls when annoyed, which is always.
flavorText: Knocked a glass off the table. The glass was also a ghost. The mess was, regrettably, real.
effect: The holder has a translucent feline that phases through barriers, senses unseen things, and helps strictly when it feels like it. Use it for spooky recon and comedic indifference. Never comes when called, always appears inconveniently.
artPrompt: A semi-transparent glowing cat perched on a windowsill at dusk, utterly unbothered, phasing one paw through the glass with maximum contempt for the living, tail flicking. No human. Moody blue-violet luminescence against warm dusk, crisp expressive linework, charming attitude, simple background.

name: Ghost Friend
description: The spirit of someone who didn't move on and decided to stick with you instead. They advise, haunt, scout places the living can't go, and refuse to discuss their unfinished business until it becomes everyone's problem.
flavorText: Loyal beyond death and twice as stubborn. Excellent company. Terrible at letting things go, which is sort of the whole situation.
effect: The holder has a spectral companion for counsel, intangible scouting, and otherworldly insight. Voice them as a real personality with their own arc and secrets. They can go where the living can't — and want something.
artPrompt: A translucent, gently glowing human-shaped spirit floating at a friendly conversational angle, expressive and warm, lower half trailing into mist, a faint melancholy behind the smile. Identity open and varied. Soft blue-white glow against dark, crisp gentle linework, friendly and wistful, simple background.

name: Pet Dragon
description: A young dragon that has imprinted on you — proud, hungry, dangerous, and deeply attached. It's the size of a large dog now and growing. The attachment is wonderful. The growth is a developing logistical and structural concern.
flavorText: Loves you fiercely. Sets things on fire affectionately. The line between 'cuddle' and 'incident' is thinner every week.
effect: The holder has a juvenile dragon companion — flight (eventually), fire, fierce loyalty, and a growing appetite. Powerful and impulsive like a brilliant toddler with weapons. Let its devotion and its destructiveness both escalate.
artPrompt: A puppy-sized young dragon with oversized wings and feet, gleeful expression, a small enthusiastic puff of flame escaping its mouth, perched ready to pounce. No human. Vivid scaled color with hot fire-accent, crisp characterful linework, adorable and hazardous, simple background.

name: Pocket-Sized Human
description: A fully sentient, fully articulate person, six inches tall, who lives in your pocket and has a complete inner life, opinions about your decisions, and a surprising amount of useful expertise for someone you could lose in a couch.
flavorText: Tiny in stature, enormous in commentary. Do not put them in your shirt pocket before a serious conversation.
effect: The holder has a miniature human ally — slips into tiny spaces, offers expert advice, and provides a whole personality at small scale. Voice them with full dignity and competence. Their size is access, not a punchline at their expense.
artPrompt: A tiny, dignified, fully-detailed person standing confidently on a giant open palm or a desk beside a normal-sized pencil for scale, mid-gesture, clearly mid-point. Diverse and specific. Bright clear color, crisp clean linework, charming and respectful, simple background.

name: Three-Headed Puppy
description: An adorable puppy with three heads, each a slightly different personality, all enthusiastically devoted to you. Guards what it loves with triple the alertness and triple the slobber. Naming it required a committee.
flavorText: Three times the loyalty, three times the appetite, and an absolutely heroic amount of drool. Worth every towel.
effect: The holder has a cerberus-puppy guardian — three heads meaning constant vigilance, layered reactions, and tireless protection. Voice the heads as a bickering, loving trio. Great loyal muscle with comedic internal disagreement.
artPrompt: A fluffy puppy with three expressive heads — one excited, one suspicious, one asleep — tails wagging, sitting eagerly, an oversized chew toy nearby. No human. Warm bright color, crisp rounded linework, triple the charm, simple background.

name: Unbranded Japanese Lightning Rat
description: A small, round, yellow electric rodent of unspecified and legally cautious origin. It crackles with affection and current, zaps threats, and absolutely refuses to evolve, get into any ball, or sign anything.
flavorText: Distinct from any electric rodent you may be thinking of. Legally, spiritually, and litigiously distinct. Choose it. It chooses you back.
effect: The holder has a small electric companion — stuns enemies, powers devices, radiates loyal cheer. A clear affectionate parody; play the static-y devotion and the comedic dodging of any 'capture' or 'evolution' framing.
artPrompt: A round, cheerful yellow electric rodent with rosy cheeks crackling with cartoon lightning, mid-happy-zap, arms up in joy, sparks arcing around it. No human. Bright electric yellow with blue spark-accents, crisp bouncy linework, gleeful parody charm, simple background.

name: Monster Pet
description: A dangerous exotic companion — basilisk, griffin, owlbear, mini-kraken, dealer's choice — that you have, against all advice, successfully befriended. It is a genuine threat to your enemies and a genuine threat to your furniture.
flavorText: Everyone else sees a monster. You see your buddy. Both of you are correct, which is exactly the problem.
effect: The holder has a formidable monstrous companion — real combat power, real handling challenges. Pick a creature and commit to its nature. Strong ally that complicates travel, lodging, and any attempt at being inconspicuous.
artPrompt: A fearsome hybrid beast — feathers, scales, claws, and a beak — lowering its head affectionately toward the viewer despite its obvious menace, powerful and devoted. No human. Bold dramatic color, crisp detailed linework, magnificent and dangerous, simple background.

name: Tiny Dinosaur
description: A small, vibrant, very real dinosaur that follows you with prehistoric enthusiasm. Smart, quick, and equipped with instincts honed over a hundred million years toward goals that occasionally align with yours.
flavorText: Survived an extinction event to be here, with you, eating something it found on the ground. Evolution is humbling.
effect: The holder has a clever small dinosaur companion — fast, sharp-toothed, surprisingly strategic. Voice it as an intelligent pack-predator that's decided you're pack. Great scout and scrapper with ancient, alien instincts.
artPrompt: A small colorful feathered raptor cocking its head with bright intelligence, one foot raised mid-step, vivid plumage, an alert and slightly scheming expression. No human. Vivid tropical color, crisp dynamic linework, prehistoric charm, simple background.

name: Warbot
description: A decommissioned (mostly) military robot that now follows you with single-minded protective devotion. It has significant firepower, outdated threat-assessment software, and a touching, literal interpretation of the word 'protect.'
flavorText: Programmed for war. Repurposed for you. Still occasionally classifies a vending machine as a hostile combatant.
effect: The holder commands a powerful combat robot — heavy protection and force, hampered by rigid logic and dated programming. Voice it as devoted, literal, and prone to overkill. Strong muscle that creates problems by taking orders too well.
artPrompt: A hulking but oddly endearing battle robot standing guard, one arm a heavy weapon now lowered, optical sensor glowing a gentle protective blue, battle-worn plating. No human. Industrial color with one warm sensor-glow, crisp mechanical linework, loyal-juggernaut energy, simple background.

name: Dragon Rider
description: A full-grown dragon has accepted you as rider, partner, or tolerated burden — the exact relationship remains under negotiation. Together you command the sky. Separately you are each insufferable about the arrangement.
flavorText: The dragon insists it chose you. You insist it was mutual. The dragon is, technically and annoyingly, correct.
effect: The holder is bonded to an adult dragon — flight, fire, immense power, and a proud equal partner with its own will. A scene-commanding ally; play the partnership's banter and the negotiation over who's actually in charge.
