artPrompt: A magnificent full-grown dragon with wings spread mid-launch, head turned back with regal expectation toward an empty saddle-spot, sky and clouds behind. No rider shown — the bond is implied. Epic jewel-tone color against bright sky, crisp powerful linework, majestic partnership, simple background.

name: Kraken
description: An enormous sea monster of legend that regards you, specifically, as a friend. It surfaces when called, drags down what threatens you, and is the single largest logistical complication ever to express genuine affection.
flavorText: Loyal, vast, and oceanic. Helpful at sea. A profound civic and zoning problem absolutely everywhere else.
effect: The holder can summon a colossal kraken in or near water — overwhelming force, ship-crushing power, devoted loyalty. Enormous and situational; let its scale solve and complicate in equal measure. It cannot be subtle.
artPrompt: A massive kraken rising from churning sea, enormous tentacles framing the scene, a single huge intelligent eye fixed warmly forward, spray and foam everywhere. No human. Deep ocean blues and greens with one warm eye-glow, crisp epic linework, awesome and affectionate, simple background.

name: Luck Dragon
description: A long, serpentine, cloud-white dragon with the temperament of a very large dog that has decided you are acceptable. It flies without visible means of propulsion and declines, politely, to explain how.
flavorText: Improbably good things keep happening near it. It takes no credit, accepts no blame, and would like to be scratched behind its improbable ears.
effect: The dragon bends probability gently toward the holder — lucky breaks, narrow escapes, the right thing at the right moment. Don't make it a problem-solver; make it the reason problems solve themselves. Loyal, serene, faintly smug.
artPrompt: A graceful cloud-white serpentine dragon coiling weightlessly through bright sky, expressive friendly eyes, flowing fur-like mane, no wings, faintly smug about the good luck trailing behind. No rider. Luminous soft color against brilliant blue, crisp flowing linework, joyful wonder, simple background.

name: Prime Dragon Companion
description: An elder dragon — ancient, vast, world-shaping — treats you as family. It has seen empires rise and bore of them, and it has decided you are interesting enough to protect, which is the highest honor it has ever conferred.
flavorText: Old enough to remember the first kingdoms. Powerful enough to end the current ones. Fond enough of you to consider it.
effect: The holder is bonded to a primordial elder dragon of immense power and ancient wisdom. A world-altering ally that may reshape kingdoms on the holder's behalf. Use its scale and history sparingly; its affection is genuine and terrifying.
artPrompt: An immense ancient dragon, scales like mountain ridges and eyes deep with millennia, curled protectively around a small empty space, weathered and majestic, faint glow of old power. No figure shown. Rich aged-gold and deep shadow, crisp grand linework, primordial and tender, simple background.

name: Cosmic Whale
description: A whale the size of a small moon that swims through space itself, bioluminescent constellations drifting across its flanks. It carries you between worlds with serene indifference to the smallness of your problems.
flavorText: Sings in a frequency that rearranges your priorities. Does not notice you the way you notice it, and that's somehow comforting.
effect: The holder travels with a vast space-faring whale — interstellar transport, awe, and a sense of cosmic scale that recontextualizes the stakes. Use it for wonder and journeys; it is benevolent, immense, and gently beyond comprehension.
artPrompt: An enormous serene whale drifting through deep space, glowing constellations and nebulae mapped across its skin, tiny stars scattered around its fins, impossibly vast and calm. No human. Deep cosmic blues and purples with starlight glow, crisp majestic linework, sublime tranquility, simple background.

name: Familiar of the Old Gods
description: A small, unassuming creature — a cat, a toad, a moth — that is in fact an avatar and conduit of powers older than language. It runs errands for you. The errands occasionally involve the fabric of reality. It seems fine.
flavorText: Looks like a pet. Answers to things that predate the sun. The casualness is the most unsettling part.
effect: The holder has a deceptively ordinary familiar channeling ancient cosmic power — minor in form, vast in potential. Voice the gap between its mundane shape and its eldritch backing. It helps quietly, with occasional reality-bending overflow.
artPrompt: A small ordinary-looking creature — a black cat or toad — sitting calmly, but with too many faintly-glowing eyes and a halo of subtle impossible geometry rippling behind it, deeply unbothered. No human. Muted natural tones with eerie violet eye-glow, crisp clean linework, mundane-meets-eldritch, simple background.

name: Pocket Universe That Purrs
description: A small, warm, contained pocket universe you can hold in your hands. It has its own stars, its own tiny physics, and — for reasons no cosmologist will explain — it purrs when content and you scratch it right.
flavorText: An entire reality, fits in a coat pocket, demands affection. The civilizations inside are presumably having a wonderful time.
effect: The holder carries a tiny, affectionate, self-contained universe — a source of wonder, refuge, raw cosmic material, and unexpected emotional companionship. Use it for awe and for the surreal comedy of a cosmos that wants belly rubs.
artPrompt: A small glowing orb cupped in open hands containing a complete swirling galaxy with tiny stars and nebulae, radiating contented warmth, faint purr-ripples on its surface. No face. Deep cosmic color with warm inner glow, crisp delicate linework, wondrous and cozy, simple background.

name: Cousin Who Knows a Guy
description: You have a cousin. The cousin knows a guy. The guy can get it — whatever it is — for a price that's reasonable and a timeline that's flexible and a story you'll never fully understand.
flavorText: You don't know the guy. You don't need to know the guy. That's the entire point of the cousin.
effect: When invoked, the holder remembers a cousin who can connect them to someone with the exact resource, contact, or favor the scene needs. Keep it small, slightly shady, and resolved off-page with a wink.
artPrompt: A warm, slightly conspiratorial figure leaning in mid-phone-call, hand cupped over the receiver, eyebrows raised like they're already sorting it out, a cluttered family-kitchen energy behind them. One of many possible people, varied and specific. Bold warm color, crisp expressive linework, knowing charm, simple background.

name: Friendly Bartender
description: A bartender who genuinely likes you. They remember your order, your last three crises, and the names of people you've forgotten. The bar is a sanctuary and they are its quietly all-knowing keeper.
flavorText: Pours a steady drink and dispenses advice you didn't ask for and will eventually realize you needed.
effect: The holder has a reliable ally behind a bar — safe harbor, local gossip, a sympathetic ear, and a knack for knowing exactly who just walked in. Use them for information, refuge, and grounding warmth.
artPrompt: A welcoming bartender mid-pour behind a glowing bar, dishtowel over one shoulder, attentive knowing expression, bottles gleaming behind them. Deliberately varied in age, build, and background. Rich amber-and-wood color, crisp warm linework, sanctuary atmosphere, simple background.

name: Guy at the Playground
description: A guy. He's at the playground. He's always at the playground. He knows who moved in, who moved out, which swing is broken, who to call. Nobody knows his name and it's too late to ask now.
flavorText: Not the mayor. Somehow more powerful than the mayor. Definitely more available.
effect: When invoked, the holder knows a guy with exactly the right hyperlocal knowledge or minor connection for the situation. He helps, usually free, always with unsolicited commentary. Keep it oddly specific.
artPrompt: A relaxed figure of ambiguous age sprawled on a park bench near playground equipment, mid-anecdote, paper cup raised for emphasis, radiating 'I know everybody' energy. Specific and varied, not a default. Warm saturated palette, crisp expressive linework, big neighborhood personality, simple background.

name: Henchman
description: A loyal, willing, moderately competent follower who carries your gear, backs your plays, and believes in you slightly more than the evidence supports. Their enthusiasm consistently outpaces their planning.
flavorText: Will absolutely take a hit for you. May also be the reason the hit was necessary.
effect: The holder gains a devoted but fallible follower — muscle, errands, a second pair of hands. Voice them as eager and sincere; let their well-meaning incompetence generate as many situations as it solves.
artPrompt: A burly, eager follower standing at the ready, slightly too keen, gear clutched in both arms, expression of pure loyal optimism, a faint dent in their helmet. Varied build and background. Bold dynamic color, crisp characterful linework, lovable-goon energy, simple background.

name: Loyal Roommate
description: A roommate who is unfailingly on your side. They cover for you, feed your weird pet, lie convincingly to people looking for you, and ask remarkably few questions about the thing in the bathtub.
flavorText: Has seen everything, judged none of it, and still pays rent on time. A genuine miracle of cohabitation.
effect: The holder has a trustworthy home-base ally — alibis, logistics, a safe place to return to, and unconditional low-drama support. Use them for cover stories and the comfort of someone who simply has your back.
artPrompt: A relaxed figure in cozy clothes on a worn couch, holding two mugs, offering one outward with an easy 'no questions' smile, a comfortably messy apartment around them. Diverse and warm. Soft saturated color, crisp friendly linework, homey loyalty, simple background.

name: True Love
description: Someone loves you, completely and specifically, and you love them back. It is the strongest and most inconvenient force in your life. It makes you braver, softer, and dramatically easier to motivate or threaten.
flavorText: Your greatest strength and your most obvious weakness, wearing the same face.
effect: The holder has a beloved whose existence raises the emotional stakes of everything — fuel for courage and sacrifice, leverage for the cruel. Let love drive bold and foolish choices in equal measure.
artPrompt: Two hands almost touching across a small gap, charged with warmth, soft light blooming between the fingertips, the rest of the world falling away into gentle blur. No faces required, identity open. Tender rose-and-gold color, crisp delicate linework, aching warmth, simple background.

name: "Daddy's" Credit Card
description: A heavy metal credit card that isn't yours, with a limit that appears to be theoretical. Someone with more money than boundaries handed it over. The bill goes somewhere else. The consequences, eventually, do not.
flavorText: Buys almost anything. Cannot buy the conversation that's coming when the statement arrives.
effect: The holder can purchase nearly anything, instantly, on someone else's tab. Pure enabling power for a scene — let the spending solve problems lavishly while the looming reckoning hums quietly in the background.
artPrompt: A gleaming heavy black metal credit card hovering and catching the light dramatically, faint embossed lettering, a small aura of reckless possibility around it. No figure. Sleek black-and-gold with one hot accent, crisp clean linework, glamorous and faintly ominous, simple background.

name: Fated Rival
description: A recurring adversary who mirrors you — equally skilled, equally stubborn, equally unwilling to let it go. They are not quite an enemy. They are the person who keeps making everything personal, and you let them.
flavorText: You sharpen each other. Neither of you will admit you'd be a little lost without the other.
effect: The holder has a destined rival who reappears to complicate, challenge, and occasionally inadvertently help. Use them for escalating one-upmanship and the grudging respect underneath. Never fully resolve it.
artPrompt: Two opposing silhouettes facing off across a charged space, mirror-posed, a crackle of rivalry-energy arcing between them, each one a different color. Identities open and varied. Bold complementary-color clash, crisp dynamic linework, electric tension, simple background.

name: Identical Twin
description: Somewhere out there is a person with your exact face. Same features, different life, occasionally overlapping enemies. They can stand in for you, ruin your reputation, or show up at the worst conceivable moment.
flavorText: Two of you. One alibi. An infinite supply of mistaken-identity problems neither of you ordered.
effect: The holder has a look-alike who can be impersonated, blamed, swapped in, or stumbled upon. Mine the confusion — alibis, doppelgänger mix-ups, and the unsettling question of which one is which.
artPrompt: Two figures in identical poses facing each other like a living mirror, one subtly different detail breaking the symmetry, a faint doubling-blur between them. Identity open, fully matched. Cool dramatic color with one warm tell, crisp clean linework, uncanny and playful, simple background.

name: Obsessive Fan
description: Someone adores you. Profoundly. Encyclopedically. They know your schedule better than you do and appear at inconvenient moments radiating devotion. Useful in a pinch. Unnerving in literally every other circumstance.
flavorText: Would do anything for you. That's the comforting part. That's also the terrifying part. Same part.
effect: The holder has a devoted superfan who provides surprising help, resources, and inside knowledge — at the cost of boundaries and the constant low hum of being watched. Play the help and the unease together.
artPrompt: A starry-eyed figure clutching memorabilia, leaning in from the edge of frame with overwhelming adoration, a wall of obsessive collage glowing behind them. Varied and specific. Vivid intense color, crisp characterful linework, devoted-and-unsettling energy, simple background.

name: A Horde of Lawyers
description: A swarm of impeccably dressed attorneys who descend on any problem with briefcases, billable hours, and terrifying procedural competence. They cannot fight. They can make fighting you a five-year financial catastrophe.
flavorText: The most powerful weapon ever deployed: a clause. They have many clauses. They are not afraid to use them.
effect: The holder summons an overwhelming legal team that resolves conflicts through paperwork, threats of litigation, and bureaucratic siege. Best used to defeat problems that can't be punched. Devastating and very slow.
artPrompt: A tight phalanx of identical sharp-suited figures advancing in formation, briefcases raised like shields, documents fanning out before them like a paper wave. Deliberately diverse within the uniform look. Crisp monochrome suits with one legal-gold accent, clean linework, ominously corporate, simple background.

name: Angelic Patron
description: A radiant higher power has taken an interest in you. It shields you, guides you, and occasionally requires you to make the morally correct choice at the most inconvenient possible time, as a condition of continued service.
flavorText: Protects you fiercely. Also keeps a meticulous ledger of your choices. The protection and the ledger are the same thing.
effect: The holder is backed by a benevolent celestial force offering protection and guidance, conditional on upholding its values. Use it for last-minute saves and moral pressure — the help comes with expectations.
artPrompt: A vast luminous angelic presence rendered as wings, geometry, and warm light rather than a literal person, leaning protectively over an empty space, radiant and watchful. No mortal figure. Brilliant gold-and-white with soft blue, crisp radiant linework, sublime and a little stern, simple background.

name: Demonic Patron
description: A powerful infernal entity backs your play. It delivers exactly what you need, when you need it, with unsettling reliability — and keeps immaculate records, because the whole arrangement runs on what you'll owe later.
flavorText: Always picks up. Always helps. Always, always remembers. The interest compounds in a currency you can't see.
effect: The holder has a dependable dark benefactor granting power and aid on credit, with a mounting unspecified debt. Let the help be genuinely effective and the cost loom — the bill is a plot engine, not a punchline.
artPrompt: An elegant infernal presence suggested through smoke, embers, and curling horned shadow rather than gore, extending a glowing helpful hand from the dark, faintly amused. No mortal figure. Deep crimson-and-black with ember-orange glow, crisp clean linework, suave and dangerous, simple background.

name: Genie
description: A wish-granting entity of enormous power and enormous theatrical commitment. It grants what you ask with flair, precision, and a connoisseur's appreciation for the loopholes you left open. The phrasing is always the battle.
flavorText: Grants the wish exactly as worded. Reads the wording exactly as it pleases. You are negotiating with a very old comedian.
effect: The holder gets wishes granted with dramatic flourish and technically-faithful mischief. Make wishes work *and* twist in equal measure. The comedy and tension both live in how the request was phrased.
artPrompt: A grand, charismatic genie billowing up from swirling colored smoke, arms spread mid-flourish, an enormous knowing grin, the lower half trailing off into vapor. Bold and theatrical, identity playful and non-default. Vivid violet-and-gold smoke, crisp dynamic linework, showman energy, simple background.

name: Private Henchman Squad
description: A small loyal team of followers — muscle, lookout, driver, and one whose role nobody can quite define. They can guard, scout, intimidate, and execute a plan with roughly seventy percent of the competence you hoped for.
flavorText: Four people, one shared brain cell, and a truly heroic amount of enthusiasm. They will get it done. Define 'it' loosely.
effect: The holder commands a small crew for coordinated tasks — heists, guarding, distractions. Strength in numbers, weakness in execution. Let plans mostly work while one member reliably introduces a fresh complication.
artPrompt: A ragtag crew of four distinct figures posed like a team about to do something ill-advised, each with a different specialty and a different vibe, one already doing something slightly wrong. Diverse in every axis. Bold dynamic color, crisp characterful linework, lovable-crew energy, simple background.

name: Sugar Momma
description: A wealthy, generous benefactor who delights in funding your life and adventures, asking little in return but attention and good company. The money is real, the affection is real, and the dynamic is more complicated than either admits.
flavorText: Bankrolls the whole operation cheerfully. The only true cost is showing up and being delightful, which is harder than it sounds.
effect: The holder has a lavish patron providing money, access, and connections in exchange for companionship and charm. Use it as an enabling resource with a relationship to maintain — generosity has its own quiet expectations.
artPrompt: An elegant, confident, well-appointed figure reclining amid luxury, gesturing generously toward the viewer as if to say 'it's handled,' radiating warmth and means. Varied, glamorous, non-stereotyped. Rich jewel-tone color with gold, crisp polished linework, opulent and warm, simple background.

name: Arch-Henchman
description: Your right hand. Terrifyingly competent, unshakably loyal, and motivated by reasons they have never fully disclosed. They anticipate your needs, cover your gaps, and are quietly better at your job than you are.
flavorText: The most capable person in the room is standing slightly behind you, by choice, for reasons you've decided not to examine.
effect: The holder has an elite, devoted lieutenant who executes flawlessly and fills every gap. A genuinely powerful ally; let their mysterious motives and superior competence quietly raise the question of who's really running things.
artPrompt: A poised, impeccably capable figure standing one respectful step back, hands clasped, utterly composed, a faint unreadable expression suggesting depths nobody has mapped. Sharp, varied, dignified. Cool sophisticated color with one warm accent, crisp clean linework, quietly formidable, simple background.

name: IOU from a Goddess
description: A goddess owes you a favor — one favor, properly earned, divinely binding. She will honor it without question when called. The trouble is that a goddess's idea of 'a favor' operates at a scale that flattens cities.
flavorText: One free miracle, redeemable anytime. Redeeming it responsibly is a skill nobody has ever demonstrated.
effect: The holder can call in a single guaranteed divine favor. It will be granted generously and at overwhelming scale. One-time use; let it solve the problem spectacularly and create a larger, more interesting one.
artPrompt: A glowing celestial token or coin etched with divine sigils, suspended in radiant light, impossibly heavy with promise, faint feminine cosmic energy haloing it. No figure. Brilliant gold-and-iris color, crisp luminous linework, sacred and weighty, simple background.

name: The President Owes You One
description: The most powerful elected official you can name owes you, personally, a favor — and remembers it. One phone call routes around armies, agencies, and the ordinary rules. Using it leaves a mark on the historical record.
flavorText: The call gets answered on the first ring. Everything after the first ring becomes someone else's classified problem.
effect: The holder can invoke a one-time favor from the highest seat of worldly power — opening doors, deploying resources, overriding bureaucracy. Enormous reach within mortal limits; the political fallout is part of the cost.
artPrompt: An ornate red telephone glowing on a desk in a seat of power, a single line lit up, heavy curtains and authority implied around it, the receiver practically humming with consequence. No figure. Deep red-and-gold with official navy, crisp clean linework, weighty and dramatic, simple background.

name: A God Is In Your Debt
description: An actual deity owes you a favor. You did something for them once — saved a temple, fed a stranger who was Them in disguise, won a bet you shouldn't have. They haven't forgotten. Gods don't.
flavorText: Cashing it in is easy. Surviving the scale of the response is the genuinely hard part.
effect: Once invoked, a real divine intervention reshapes the scene — but gods are imprecise and operate at scales that don't fit the situation. Narrate help that vastly overshoots the problem, gloriously and consequentially.
artPrompt: A vast luminous divine presence of light, sacred geometry, and many warm attentive eyes filling the frame — not humanoid — leaning in, genuinely pleased to help and about to wildly overdo it. No mortal figure. Radiant gold-and-jewel cosmic color, crisp clean radiant linework, sublime warmth, simple background.

name: Death Owes You a Favor
description: Death — the actual one, robe optional — owes you. You cheated it, charmed it, or did it an unexpected kindness, and now it carries a quiet debt. Death always pays. Death's idea of payment is, necessarily, complicated.
flavorText: The one entity with perfect attendance owes you a day off. Scheduling it is going to be a conversation.
effect: The holder can call in a favor from Death itself — a stay of mortality, a soul returned, a door reopened. Profound and one-time; honor it with weight and a wry, ancient courtesy. Death is fair, not generous.
artPrompt: A tall, calm, hooded presence rendered with quiet dignity rather than horror, extending a single skeletal hand palm-up in a gesture of fair repayment, a faint warm light in the dark of the hood. No mortal figure. Muted greys and bone-white with one warm glow, crisp clean linework, solemn and oddly gentle, simple background.

name: The Author Likes You
description: The being writing this story is, against all professional detachment, fond of you. Coincidences break your way. Cuts heal between scenes. The narrative bends, just slightly, to keep you interesting and alive.
flavorText: You are the favorite. It's wonderful until you remember that authors adore putting their favorites through absolute hell.
effect: The holder enjoys narrative favor — lucky timing, soft landings, plot conveniences that keep them in the story. Meta and powerful; let it save them in stylish ways while the affection occasionally manifests as 'interesting' suffering.
artPrompt: A warm beam of golden 'spotlight' light falling from an unseen source onto an empty marked spot on a stage, a faint quill-and-ink shimmer in the air above, the world arranging itself favorably around that single lit space. No figure. Warm gold against soft dark, crisp clean linework, meta and tender, simple background.

name: Generate Light
description: You can conjure light from nothing — a warm glow, a sharp beam, a gentle drifting orb. It is the first spell everyone learns and the one that saves your life most often, which nobody mentions in the brochure.
flavorText: Not flashy. Not legendary. The single most useful thing to have when the lamp goes out and something starts breathing.
effect: The holder summons light of any size or color at will. Humble but constantly useful — banish darkness, signal, reveal, comfort. Let its modesty be the point in tense or spooky moments.
artPrompt: A small radiant orb of warm light hovering in cupped emptiness, pushing back a circle of soft darkness, gentle rays catching dust motes in the air. No figure. Glowing gold-and-amber against deep blue shadow, crisp clean linework, quietly hopeful, simple background.

name: Make Self Forgettable
description: A minor enchantment that makes you deeply, profoundly unremarkable. People look right at you and their attention slides off like rain on wax. Useful for leaving rooms. Devastating for first dates.
flavorText: You were there the whole time. Nobody will swear to it in court.
effect: The holder becomes easy to overlook and hard to recall for a scene — people see them but don't register them. Not invisibility. Great for slipping away; quietly sad for everything else. Lean into both.
artPrompt: A figure dissolving into pleasant invisibility — rendered in soft grey and half-finished lines while the vivid, sharp, colorful world carries on indifferently around the fading shape, one wistful little wave. Desaturated subject against bold color, crisp linework, gentle melancholy with wit, simple background.

name: Minor Charm
description: A small persuasive glamour. People find you a touch more agreeable, a shade more trustworthy, slightly more likely to give you the good table. It won't win a war. It will win a parking spot.
flavorText: Not mind control. More like the universe's complimentary mint. Small, pleasant, and gone before anyone notices.
effect: The holder is mildly, magically likable for a scene — smoothing minor social friction, nudging small favors. Keep it subtle and deniable; it greases situations rather than overpowering anyone.
artPrompt: A soft pink-gold shimmer of charm radiating outward in gentle heart-light and sparkle, melting a frowning doorway or gate into a welcoming one. No figure. Warm rose-and-gold palette, crisp clean linework, inviting and a little cheeky, simple background.

name: Find Lost Objects
description: You always know where the missing thing is. Keys, rings, the other sock, the political prisoner, the meaning you misplaced years ago — the spell points you straight to it, with no comment on whether you'll like the trip.
flavorText: Knows where it is. Declines to mention why it's there or what's guarding it. The spell is helpful, not kind.
effect: The holder can locate any specific lost object or person they focus on. Use it to find the plot-critical thing — then let getting to it be the actual adventure.
artPrompt: A glowing dowsing-thread of light spiraling through a cluttered dim space toward a single softly pulsing lost object half-hidden in shadow. No figure. Cool indigo space with one warm beacon-glow, crisp clean linework, mysterious and guiding, simple background.

name: Mend Broken Things
description: You can fix what's broken — a cracked cup, a snapped blade, a shattered keepsake — knitting it whole with a touch. It works on objects beautifully. On feelings and bones it gets ambitious and unreliable.
flavorText: Restores the object perfectly. Whether the object wanted to be restored is a separate and unasked question.
effect: The holder repairs broken physical objects with a touch, good as new. Use for clever fixes and emotional beats around restored keepsakes; it falters on living tissue and metaphors.
artPrompt: A shattered ceramic cup reassembling in midair, glowing golden seams racing along the cracks kintsugi-style, the final shard clicking into place. No figure. Warm gold-on-white with deep accent shadows, crisp clean linework, satisfying and tender, simple background.

name: Pocket Familiar
description: A tiny magical companion that lives in your pocket, sleeve, or hat — a wisp, a sprite, a small grumpy spark with eyes. It offers advice, occasional spellwork, and relentless commentary on your decisions.
flavorText: Pocket-sized. Mood: enormous. Has been alive far longer than you and is not impressed by your plan.
effect: The holder has a small magical familiar offering minor spells, lore, and unsolicited opinions. Voice it as a tiny, ancient, judgmental ally — helpful, but never quietly.
artPrompt: A tiny glowing sprite with oversized expressive eyes perched on the rim of a coat pocket, arms crossed, radiating small-but-mighty attitude, faint magical sparks drifting around it. No human face shown. Vivid jewel-tone color, crisp characterful linework, comic and charming, simple background.

name: Book of Unwritten Stories
description: A book whose pages are blank until you start reading, at which point a story writes itself ahead of your eyes — and once written, it has a habit of coming true somewhere, to someone, soon.
flavorText: Every story it tells is real. The trick is making sure it's telling a story you'd want to live near.
effect: The holder reads (and thereby authors) a new story that begins manifesting in the world. Use it to seed plot, summon situations, or write a way out — with the risk the tale runs longer than intended.
artPrompt: An open book with luminous ink flowing onto blank pages on its own, words lifting off and blossoming into small three-dimensional scenes above the paper. No figure. Warm parchment-gold with magical blue ink-light, crisp clean linework, wondrous and a little eerie, simple background.

name: Cursed Ring
description: A beautiful ring that grants real power and exacts a quiet, escalating price. You'll notice the power immediately. You'll notice the price later, when removing the ring becomes a topic the ring would rather not discuss.
flavorText: It was a gift. Most curses are. That's how they get in the door.
effect: The ring grants a genuine boon shadowed by a creeping cost or compulsion. Let the power be useful and the curse unfold slowly. Taking it off should be its own escalating problem.
artPrompt: An ornate golden ring resting on dark velvet, glowing with seductive inner light, faint shadowy tendrils curling possessively up from it like smoke testing the air. No figure. Rich gold-and-crimson against black, crisp ornate linework, beautiful and ominous, simple background.

name: Dreamcatcher Hat
description: A hat woven with a dreamcatcher's web that filters the wearer's thoughts and the room's. It snags nightmares, omens, and the occasional useful premonition, holding them in its threads like dew.
flavorText: Catches dreams. Does not sort them. You'll have to check the web yourself, and some of what's stuck there is staring back.
effect: The hat grants the wearer prophetic dreams and protection from psychic or nightmare effects, with caught omens readable in its web. Use it to deliver cryptic hints and ward off mental intrusion.
artPrompt: A soft slouchy hat crowned with an intricate glowing dreamcatcher web, tiny luminous dream-fragments and one dark snagged nightmare tangled in its threads, feathers drifting. No figure. Twilight purples and warm gold glow, crisp clean linework, mystical and gentle, simple background.

name: Infinite Acorn
description: An ordinary-looking acorn that, when planted, grows whatever is most needed — a tree, a ladder, a shelter, a distraction, a forest the size of an argument — instantly and enthusiastically.
flavorText: From tiny acorns, mighty and contextually-appropriate solutions grow. Usually. The acorn improvises.
effect: Planting the acorn instantly grows exactly what the situation needs, in plant form. Reward creative use — bridges, cover, escape routes — and let the acorn occasionally interpret 'needed' with botanical liberty.
artPrompt: A single glowing acorn dropped onto soil, an explosive upward burst of luminous green growth erupting from it — vines, branches, and leaves spiraling into a useful improbable shape. No figure. Vivid emerald-and-gold against earthy brown, crisp clean linework, joyful surge of life, simple background.

name: Sinister Puzzle Box
description: An intricate box that wants to be opened, and rewards opening, and should under no circumstances be opened. All three of these are true at once. The craftsmanship is genuinely exquisite.
flavorText: Contains a prize. Contains a price. The box is confident you'll convince yourself the first outweighs the second.
effect: The box tempts the holder to solve it for a reward, with a lurking consequence. Use the slow dread of the solving; deliver something genuinely useful tangled with something genuinely unwise.
artPrompt: An intricate black-and-gold geometric puzzle box, panels half-shifted mid-solve, an unsettling sliver of red light leaking from a seam, the carvings almost moving. No figure. Deep blacks and golds with one menacing red glow, crisp meticulous linework, beautiful and wrong, simple background.

name: Flying Carpet
description: A magnificent woven carpet that flies, banks, and hovers on command. It is faster than it looks, prouder than it should be, and deeply opinionated about being rolled up for storage.
flavorText: Centuries of craftsmanship and zero seatbelts. Hold the tassels and trust the textile.
effect: The carpet carries the holder and companions through the air with style and a mind of its own. Great for travel and chases; give it personality — it sulks, it shows off, it has favorite routes.
artPrompt: An ornate tasseled carpet soaring and banking through open sky, edges rippling with the wind, intricate woven patterns glowing faintly, clearly enjoying itself. No riders needed. Rich jewel-tone reds and golds against bright blue, crisp ornate linework, joyful and proud, simple background.

name: Magic Lamp
description: An old brass lamp with a resident of negotiable cooperation. Rub it and something powerful and theatrical emerges, ready to grant wishes within terms it will define generously for itself and narrowly for you.
flavorText: Three wishes. Infinite loopholes. Read the room before you phrase the request, because the lamp certainly will.
effect: The lamp produces a wish-granting entity that fulfills requests with maximum technically-correct mischief. Make wishes work and backfire in equal, entertaining measure. Phrasing is everything.
artPrompt: A weathered brass oil lamp on sand, a great plume of luminous colored smoke erupting from its spout and coiling into the suggestion of an enormous grinning presence. No human. Warm brass-and-violet smoke against night-blue, crisp clean linework, magical and sly, simple background.

name: Potion of Randomness
description: A swirling, color-shifting potion that does something. Nobody can say what. The label fell off, the alchemist retired in a hurry, and the only honest description is 'an effect, probably temporary, possibly yours.'
flavorText: Could heal you. Could turn you into a competent jazz pianist. Could summon a goose. The potion is committed to surprise.
effect: Drinking it produces a completely random, scene-appropriate magical effect — helpful, harmful, or bizarre. Roll the dice on tone and commit. Best deployed when the holder is desperate enough to gamble.
artPrompt: A glass vial of swirling liquid that can't decide on a color, shifting through the whole spectrum mid-bubble, faint question-mark wisps of vapor rising, a discarded blank label beside it. No figure. Riotous shifting rainbow against neutral wood, crisp clean linework, playful chaos, simple background.

name: Deck of Many Things
description: A deck of cards where each card rewrites a piece of your reality — fortune, ruin, a new ally, a sudden enemy, the loss of something you forgot you had. You draw at your own risk. Everyone does. Everyone keeps drawing.
flavorText: The single most dangerous thing a bored adventurer can hold. The boredom always wins.
effect: Drawing a card triggers a major, random reality-altering event — wildly good or catastrophic. Use it for dramatic swings the holder can't control. The temptation to draw 'just one more' is the trap.
artPrompt: A fanned spread of ornate arcana-style cards hovering in midair, each one glowing with a different fate — a crown, a skull, a key, a falling tower — cards lifting and swirling with palpable danger. No figure. Vivid jewel-tones with gold leaf against dark, crisp ornate linework, fateful and electric, simple background.

name: Monkey's Paw
description: A withered severed paw that grants wishes with malicious literalism. It gives you exactly what you asked for and nothing you meant, curling one finger with each granted wish, savoring the gap.
flavorText: The wish comes true. So does everything you failed to specify. It's the specifying that gets you.
effect: Each wish is granted in the most technically-faithful, spiritually-devastating way possible. Honor the letter, betray the intent. Classic ironic-twist engine — the holder gets what they said, never what they wanted.
artPrompt: A dried, curled monkey's paw resting on dark stone, one finger mid-curl, a sickly green glow pulsing from within it as if savoring something. No figure. Muted browns and bone-white with eerie green light, crisp detailed linework, grim and gleeful, simple background.

name: Necronomicon
description: A book of forbidden knowledge bound in materials the publisher declines to itemize. It contains real power and real answers, each one slightly too large for the human mind to hold without some spillage.
flavorText: Every page is a door. Most of them open inward. A few open onto things that were content where they were.
effect: The book grants forbidden spells and cosmic truths at a cost to sanity, safety, or the fabric of the scene. Let knowledge be genuinely powerful and genuinely unwise. Reading aloud is always a mistake worth making.
artPrompt: An ancient tome bound in dark cracked leather, clasps straining, sickly green-and-violet light bleeding from its edges, eldritch sigils writhing across the cover, the air around it visibly wrong. No figure. Deep shadow with toxic green-violet glow, crisp ornate linework, forbidden and alive, simple background.

name: Magic Spellbook
description: The spellbook — the complete one, every spell that is or could be, rewriting itself as magic evolves. It will teach you anything. It will also judge your pronunciation, your ambition, and your handwriting.
flavorText: Contains every spell ever cast and several that are still deciding whether to exist. Indexed by a system only it understands.
effect: The holder can cast nearly any spell they can find and pronounce within the book. A top-tier, open-ended magic source. Let access feel limitless but gate it behind finding, understanding, and surviving the right page.
artPrompt: A colossal floating spellbook with pages turning on their own, luminous spells of every color lifting off the parchment and orbiting it, the book radiating ancient authority. No figure. Brilliant multicolored magic against warm parchment and deep blue, crisp intricate linework, awe-inspiring, simple background.

name: Rewrite One Law of Nature
description: The power to change one fundamental law of nature — gravity, causality, the speed of light, whether Tuesdays exist — permanently and universally. You get one. Choose as if everyone is watching, because afterward, everyone will be.
flavorText: One edit to the source code of reality. No undo. No preview. The compiler does not warn you about runtime errors.
effect: The holder permanently alters one rule of physics or reality, reshaping the entire world around that change. A world-defining, irreversible act — play out the sweeping, unforeseen consequences of the new rule.
artPrompt: A single glowing equation or rune suspended in space, mid-edit, with reality visibly reorganizing around it — objects beginning to float or fall differently, light bending into new colors, the world rewriting outward in luminous ripples. No figure. Electric cosmic spectrum against deep black, crisp clean linework, momentous and surreal, simple background.

name: Wish Engine
description: A vast, humming machine built to grant a single perfect wish. It calculates every consequence across every timeline before it acts, which is reassuring, until you realize it has its own definition of 'perfect.'
flavorText: It will grant your wish flawlessly, accounting for everything. 'Everything' is a larger category than you were budgeting for.
effect: The engine grants one fully-realized wish, optimizing across all consequences toward its own inscrutable idea of the best outcome. Make the result genuinely wondrous and genuinely not what was pictured. World-scale stakes.
artPrompt: An immense cathedral-sized machine of glowing gears, lenses, and conduits, all focused inward toward a single blinding point of pure potential at its core, reality bending slightly around it. No figure. Epic cosmic gold-and-cyan against vast dark, crisp intricate linework, sublime and overwhelming, simple background.

name: Always Lucky
description: Probability has decided it likes you. Coins land your way, lines move when you join them, toast falls butter-up. Nobody can prove it. Everybody has noticed.
flavorText: Not skill. Not destiny. Just the dice quietly rooting for you and refusing to explain why.
effect: Tilt minor outcomes in the holder's favor — small, plot-convenient strokes of fortune. Keep it modest and frequent rather than dramatic; the comedy is in how casually it works.
artPrompt: A spinning coin frozen mid-flip surrounded by a golden halo of good fortune, four-leaf clovers and tiny sparks orbiting it, the coin itself looking smug. No people. Warm saturated gold-and-green palette, crisp confident lines, cheerful energy, simple background.

name: Never Gets Lost
description: An unerring internal compass. Drop you anywhere — a maze, a foreign city, an interdimensional parking structure — and you always know exactly which way is out. Why you went in remains your problem.
flavorText: Knows the way. Has opinions about the route. Will not be taking questions.
effect: The holder always knows the correct direction out of any space, however impossible. Use it to skip navigation problems and to deadpan past labyrinths that were supposed to matter.
artPrompt: A glowing compass rose hovering in midair, its needle confident and slightly cocky, faint golden trail-lines spiraling out of an impossible maze behind it. No figure. Bold dynamic color, crisp clean linework, adventurous warmth, simple background.

name: Sixth Sense
description: A low-grade precognitive itch. You know when something's about to go wrong roughly four seconds before it does — never enough time to stop it, exactly enough to say 'oh no' with feeling.
flavorText: Comes with no instructions, no off switch, and no warranty. The universe whispers; it declines to clarify.
effect: Grant the holder a vague but accurate premonition right before a turn goes sideways. They sense the trouble and can rarely prevent it. Narrate the dread, then the confirmation.
artPrompt: A vivid burst of premonition with no person in it — concentric cyan and warning-orange shockwave ripples exploding from a single bright point, a clock caught mid-shatter, the pure visual feeling of 'oh no' arriving four seconds early. Bold dynamic color, crisp clean linework, kinetic dread, simple background.

name: Almost Invisible
description: You are not invisible. You are *almost* invisible, which is worse, because it means people keep half-seeing you and asking if you're okay. Effective in dim rooms. Useless in conversation.
flavorText: Ninety percent transparent. The remaining ten percent is always the part someone is looking at.
effect: The holder is hard to see but never fully unseen — good for sneaking until someone glances directly at the lingering ten percent. Mine the gap between 'gone' and 'mostly gone' for comedy.
artPrompt: A figure-shaped shimmer of near-transparency, faint outline catching the light, one stubbornly solid elbow or shoe still fully visible and a little embarrassed about it. Cool translucent blues against a vivid background, crisp linework, comic awkwardness, simple background.

name: Super Strength
description: You can lift the car. You can lift the bus. You can lift the question of whether you should be lifting the bus, which nobody else seems to be asking. Doorknobs fear you.
flavorText: The strength is unlimited. The structural integrity of everything you touch is not.
effect: The holder can lift, bend, or hurl anything. Let the fun come from collateral damage and the social awkwardness of being the strongest thing in any room, not from combat alone.
artPrompt: A pair of powerful arms hoisting an absurdly large object — a small bus, a boulder, an entire vending machine — overhead with ease, dynamic upward motion lines, the object slightly crumpled where gripped. Bold saturated color, crisp punchy linework, triumphant energy, simple background.

name: Talk to Animals
description: You understand every animal and they understand you. This sounds magical until you learn that most of them are complaining, and the pigeons have a lot to say about you specifically.
flavorText: Turns out the animal kingdom is one enormous group chat and it has been talking about you.
effect: The holder converses with any creature. Animals are honest, self-interested, and full of gossip and useful local knowledge. Voice them with strong, specific, slightly petty personalities.
artPrompt: A lively semicircle of wildly varied animals — a crow, a cat, a frog, a deeply unimpressed pigeon — all mid-conversation, expressive faces, speech-energy crackling between them, no human present. Vivid dynamic color, crisp characterful linework, comedic warmth, simple background.

name: Flight
description: You can fly. Properly fly — up, around, away from your problems and toward new and more aerial ones. Landing is a skill sold separately.
flavorText: Gravity filed a complaint. It was denied. It is appealing.
effect: The holder flies freely. Use it for escape, spectacle, and the specific joy of solving a grounded problem by simply leaving the ground. Weather and stamina can complicate it.
artPrompt: A dynamic upward-soaring pose silhouette dissolving into pure motion — streaming wind-lines, a triumphant arc across an open sky, cape or coat snapping, viewed from below for maximum drama. No identifiable face needed. Brilliant blue-and-gold color, crisp bold linework, exhilarating energy, simple background.

name: Invisibility (At Will)
description: Total, reliable invisibility, at will. You wink out completely — no shimmer, no outline, no ten-percent elbow. The catch is purely existential: it's lonely, being unseeable on demand.
flavorText: They can't see you. They can still hear you knock the lamp over. Stealth is a team effort and your body did not get the memo.
effect: The holder vanishes fully and at will. Strong for infiltration and escape; complicate it with sound, smell, and the quiet melancholy of being unwitnessed. Don't let it trivialize every scene.
artPrompt: An empty outfit caught mid-stride — coat, scarf, and boots arranged as if worn by absolutely nothing, one sleeve reaching for a doorhandle. Eerie and a little playful. Cool moody palette with one vivid accent, crisp clean linework, simple background.

name: Photographic Memory
description: You remember everything, perfectly, forever. Every page, every face, every excruciating thing you said in a lift in 2009. The recall is total. The delete key was never installed.
flavorText: A gift with no trash folder. You will recall this sentence on your deathbed, unfortunately.
effect: The holder perfectly recalls anything they've seen or read. Great for puzzles and continuity catches; the cost is they also can't forget the embarrassing and painful, which the scene can exploit.
artPrompt: A glowing brain-shaped library, shelves and filing drawers radiating outward into bright space, every drawer crisp and labeled, one drawer ajar leaking a single mortifying memory. Vivid color, clean crisp linework, witty and cerebral, simple background.

name: 4D Swiss Army Knife
description: A multi-tool that folds out tools from directions that don't exist. Need a corkscrew? There. Need a smaller, perpendicular yesterday? Also there. Closing it again is the real challenge.
flavorText: Forty-one functions, only nine of which occur in the three spatial dimensions you're using.
effect: The holder produces exactly the right impossible tool for the moment — including ones that manipulate time, angle, or perspective. Let it solve problems in ways that raise new, funnier problems.
artPrompt: A glinting red multi-tool with implements unfolding in impossible directions — one blade bending into a fourth axis, another casting a shadow that points the wrong way, geometry quietly breaking around it. Bold red-and-steel color, crisp precise linework, clever surreal energy, simple background.

name: Mind Reading
description: You hear what people are thinking. All of it. The polite surface thought and the screaming subtext underneath, simultaneously, at all times, including during small talk about the weather.
flavorText: Everyone is more interesting and more exhausting than they appear. Mostly exhausting.
effect: The holder hears others' true thoughts under their spoken words. Surface the gap between what characters say and mean for comedy and tension. The constant noise can also overwhelm the holder.
artPrompt: A head surrounded by a chaotic swirling cloud of overlapping thought-bubbles in clashing colors — anxieties, lunch plans, secret crushes, one bubble just screaming — no readable text, pure visual overload. Vivid dynamic color, crisp linework, comic chaos, simple background.

name: Teleportation
description: You can jump instantly to anywhere you can clearly picture. The picturing is load-bearing. Misremember the décor and you may arrive inside it.
flavorText: Instant travel, zero frequent-flyer miles, occasional fusion with the furniture. Visualize carefully.
effect: The holder blinks across space to places they know well. Use for dramatic exits and entrances; complicate with imperfect memory of the destination and the unsettling instant of being nowhere.
artPrompt: A figure mid-teleport, half-dissolved into a burst of fracturing light-shards, one side already gone, a clean pop of displaced air where the rest is vanishing. Electric violet-and-cyan color, crisp sharp linework, kinetic snap of energy, simple background.

name: Dimension Hopper
description: A device — handheld, blinking, alarmingly casual — that punches a door to the next reality over. The next reality over is rarely an upgrade, but it's reliably different, which sometimes is enough.
flavorText: Warranty void in all realities including this one. Side effects may include other selves with opinions.
effect: The holder opens a portal to a parallel reality, escaping or relocating the entire scene. Each destination is a fresh, weird premise. Great for hard resets; the way back is never guaranteed.
artPrompt: A glowing handheld device tearing a bright jagged rip in the air, through which a completely different and stranger world is visible — different sky, different physics — the device blinking innocently. No figure. Saturated rainbow-edged color, crisp clean linework, sense of vast possibility, simple background.

name: Flying Hot Tub Time Machine
description: A flying hot tub that is also a time machine. The two functions were developed separately and never fully reconciled, which is why the temporal controls are slippery when wet.
flavorText: The only time machine you can also relax in. The bubbles are not load-bearing but they help.
effect: The holder travels through time and the air in a buoyant, ridiculous hot tub. Lean fully into the absurdity — soggy paradoxes, arriving in the wrong century at the wrong temperature, group adventures.
artPrompt: A wooden hot tub soaring through a swirling time-vortex sky, steam and bubbles trailing behind it, glowing temporal dials bolted clumsily to its rim, jets of water flinging off into the void. No people needed. Warm wood tones against electric vortex color, crisp linework, joyful absurdity, simple background.

name: Matter Replicator
description: Point it at a thing, get a second identical thing. Point it at the second thing, get a third. The economy this destroys is not your problem until, suddenly and dramatically, it is.
flavorText: Scarcity was a lovely idea while it lasted. Replication does not ask permission and does not do quality control.
effect: The holder duplicates any object they can scan. Powerful and economy-breaking; let copies be subtly imperfect and let abundance create funnier problems than scarcity ever did.
artPrompt: A sleek device emitting a grid of light over an apple, with a second apple materializing in shimmering pixelated layers beside it, and a faint third apple already beginning to flicker into being. No figure. Cool tech-blue with a warm apple-red accent, crisp clean linework, simple background.

name: Time Travel
description: You move through time itself. Forward is easy and unsettling. Backward is dangerous and addictive. Every change you make ripples, and the ripples have opinions and, occasionally, lawyers.
flavorText: The past is not a museum. It is a very breakable shop, and you are holding a very large bag.
effect: The holder travels through time, with every change spawning consequences. Use for high-stakes do-overs and paradox comedy; never let a trip be clean. The timeline pushes back.
artPrompt: A grand clockwork spiral of glowing gears and calendar-pages stretching into infinity, a single bright thread weaving backward through it, small ripples of altered history blooming where it touches. No figure. Rich sepia-and-gold with electric blue accents, crisp intricate linework, epic and uneasy, simple background.

name: Watch That Freezes Time
description: A pocket watch that stops time for everyone but you. The world holds its breath; you wander through the frozen tableau doing whatever you like, aging quietly and alone in a universe on pause.
flavorText: Infinite time to think of the perfect comeback. The moment unfreezes before you can deliver it. Every time.
effect: The holder freezes time and moves freely through the stilled scene. Excellent for heists and dramatic rearrangements; touch on the loneliness of being the only thing moving for the deeper note.
artPrompt: An ornate pocket watch glowing at the center of a frozen scene — suspended raindrops, a paused splash, a leaf hanging mid-fall, everything crystalline and still around it. No figure. Cool silver-blue palette with warm watch-gold, crisp meticulous linework, serene and uncanny, simple background.

name: Button That Destroys the World
description: A single red button. It is not labeled, because the people who made it assumed the label was obvious and the people who'd press it wouldn't read it anyway. Both assumptions were correct.
flavorText: The instructions said 'do not press.' This has never once worked as a deterrent in the entire history of buttons.
effect: When used, the world — this scene, this reality, this everything — ends, catastrophically and irreversibly. Narrate the press, the silence, the escalation, and whatever absurd thing comes after the end. Don't let it be un-pressed.
artPrompt: A single fat glossy red button radiating pure menace on a stark pedestal, one hard beam of light, deep black void all around it — the most dangerous object ever rendered, and it knows it. Saturated red-on-black, crisp clean edges, ominous swagger, simple background.

name: The Script
description: The actual script of the story you're in. You can read ahead. You can scribble in the margins. You can cross out a line of dialogue and write a better one, and reality will perform your edit, grudgingly.
flavorText: Spoilers for your own life. Editing privileges included. The narrator is not happy about this and you can hear them sigh.
effect: The holder can read and rewrite the narrative itself — foreseeing or altering what happens next. The ultimate fourth-wall power. Let edits work but invite narrative pushback and unintended rewrites.
artPrompt: A glowing bound screenplay floating open, pages riffling, several lines crossed out and rewritten in luminous ink, the edited words lifting off the page to reshape the air around them. No figure. Warm parchment-and-gold with electric editing-blue, crisp clean linework, meta and magical, simple background.

name: True Resurrection
description: The power to bring back the dead — including yourself — fully, properly, no zombie loopholes. The body returns whole. The person returns mostly. Death does not enjoy being overruled and remembers faces.
flavorText: Reversible mortality. Comes with a faint and growing sense that you are running up a tab somewhere.
effect: The holder restores the dead to genuine life, themselves included. A scene-defining power; give it weight and cost — Death notices, the returned are subtly changed, and the tab eventually comes due.
artPrompt: A radiant upward surge of golden light reassembling scattered motes into the silhouette of a returning soul, dark tendrils of Death reluctantly releasing their grip at the edges. No specific face. Brilliant gold-and-white against deep shadow, crisp luminous linework, sublime and solemn, simple background.
